title: Graduate Quantity Surveyor
description: Join us to help lead the next wave of the digital economy. Our Graduate Programme is a structured two\-year programme that will equip you with the skills and experience required to have a successful career at Apto. As part of this programme, we are recruiting a **Graduate Quantity Surveyor** with a commencement date in early **September 2026** and the role is based in **Dublin, Ireland** . From day one you will gain real hands\-on experience on cutting edge data centre projects while being supported and nurtured by our expert management teams. APTO Data Centres \| What we Do. Apto is a data centre company that designs, builds, and operates hyperscale data centres for our customers in high growth and emerging markets across Europe. Apto has been formed to develop and operate the European data centre assets of global investment manager, PIMCO, benefiting from access to scale, resources and land sourcing capabilities of one of the largest European real estate platforms. Guided by an esteemed leadership team with insights drawn from the world’s digital and data centre titans, Apto brings over a century of combined experience in designing, constructing and operating data centres at scale. From Day 1, the leadership team has focused on the type of company we want to build. We measure success by how well we demonstrate our values every day. We nurture an unfaltering passion for excellence, guided by our customers’ needs and expectations. We obsessively take ownership, deliver on our promises, and create lasting positive impressions. We thrive on overcoming any hurdle to give our customers the best possible experience and outcomes. And, we have fun along the way. Our access to capital and real estate is important but our most valuable asset is our team, and we are delighted now to be recruiting for a Graduate Quantity Surveyor. The role * Support the procurement of Design, General Contractor, Owner Furnished Contractor Installed (OFCI) and third party consultant work packages. * Support the development and validation of construction estimates at various stages of design. * Maintain accurate cost databases and historical cost data. * Monitor cost performance metrics and support monthly cost reporting. * Support the construction team with project close out and final accounts. * Support the management of external consultants. * Collaborate with cross functional teams to ensure effective communication and implementation of commercial processes and procedures. **Basic Qualifications** * Degree in Quantity Surveying or equivalent qualification. Location St Stephen’s Green, Dublin 2, Ireland. Office based at least three days per week. 20% travel across Europe. Employee Benefits * Competitive Salary * Eligible for bonus and Long Term Incentive Plan (LTIP). * 25 days annual leave plus your birthday and bank holidays * Company Pension Contribution * Fully supported and funded professional membership. * Comprehensive Health Insurance for you and your dependents. Life insurance, Critical Illness Cover and Income Protection Reporting Commercial Director **Apto is an equal opportunities employer.** \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ Contact Apto today If you are interested, please apply with your CV! \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

title: Graduate software engineer
description: At Bending Spoons, we’re striving to build one of the all\-time great companies. A company that serves a huge number of customers. A company where team members grow to their full potential. A company that functions at unparalleled levels of effectiveness and efficiency. A company that creates value for shareowners at an extraordinary rate. And a company that does so while adhering to high ethical standards. In pursuit of this objective, we acquire and improve digital businesses, not to sell on, but to own and operate for the long term. The transformations we make are often deep—designed to speed up innovation, benefit customers, and strengthen business performance. Here, hierarchy is minimal and teams are small and talent\-dense. We operate established products with the ambition, agility, and urgency of a startup. Across the company, we integrate AI deeply into how we work so that human judgment and machine intelligence reinforce each other. For a talented, driven, and collaborative individual, working at Bending Spoons is an opportunity to learn, make an impact, and progress their career at an exceptionally high rate. That’s our promise to such a candidate. **A few examples of your responsibilities** * **Build software that matters.** Take real ownership from idea to production, creating systems used by millions and evolving them into products at scale. * **Amplify your impact with AI.** Integrate the most powerful AI tools directly into your development workflow—design, implementation, testing, and documentation—to move faster while maintaining high standards for correctness, reliability, and maintainability. * **Master your toolkit.** Work across diverse stacks with end\-to\-end ownership, choosing the right technologies for each challenge. From monoliths to microservices, gRPC to REST, Kubernetes to Docker, Python to Rust—you’ll apply technologies thoughtfully, focusing on depth and purpose rather than trends. * **Simplify relentlessly.** Question every layer of complexity. Improve architectures, pipelines, and codebases to build systems that are simpler, more scalable, and easier to maintain. **What we look for** * **Reasoning ability.** Given the necessary knowledge, you can solve complex problems. You think from first principles, and structure your ideas sharply. You resist the influence of biases. You identify and take care of the details that matter. * **Drive.** You’re extremely ambitious in everything you do—and your initiative, effort, and tenacity match the intensity of your ambition. You feel deeply responsible for your work. You hold yourself to a high—and rising—bar. * **Team spirit.** You give generously and without the expectation of receiving in return. You support the best idea, not your idea. You're always happy to get your hands dirty to help your team. You’re reliable, honest, and transparent. * **Proficiency in English.** You read, write, and speak proficiently in English. **What we offer** * **Incredibly talented, entrepreneurial teams.** You’ll work in small, result\-oriented, autonomous teams alongside some of the brightest people in your field. * **An exceptional opportunity for growth.** We go to great lengths to hire individuals of outstanding potential—then, our priority is to put them in the ideal position to thrive. Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. And if you’ve got what it takes, you’ll soon be playing an essential role in major projects, too. * **Competitive pay and access to equity in the company.** Typically, we offer individuals at the start of their career an annual salary of £85,797 in London and €66,065 elsewhere in Europe. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £112,189 and £250,512 in London, and €107,837 and €188,848 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut. * **All. These. Benefits.** Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top\-of\-the\-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work. * **A flexible start date and part\-time options.** You don't need to wait until graduation to apply. We offer flexible start dates and the possibility to begin part\-time, transitioning to full\-time as you complete your degree. Many Spooners joined before graduating and progressively took on greater responsibility, with arrangements that allowed them to do so without compromising their education. **Commitment \& contract** Permanent or fixed\-term. Full\-time. **Location** Milan (Italy), London (UK), Madrid (Spain), Warsaw (Poland) or remote in selected countries. **The selection process** In our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self\-reject. If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours. We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times. We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form. **Before you apply** If you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again. Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learning, achievement, and career growth, it also requires significant commitment. To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we recommend spending your first few months working from our Milan office, regardless of your long\-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from our offices in Milan or London, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage. If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

title: Group Financial Accountant
description: **Job Title: Group Financial Accountant** **Reports to: Financial Controller** **Location: Head Office, Drogheda, Co Louth** **Role Overview:** The Financial Accountant role is a critical position in the Finance team which will report to the Financial Controller \& work closely with other members of the senior management team. A key part of this highly visible role is to provide business data and analysis to managers within the organisation to assist in business decision\-making and control and to summarise and present key issues as they arise. We are looking for someone who is naturally curious, thrives on solving problems and is eager to make a real impact on our business. **Main Job Tasks and Responsibilities:** * Preparing timely and accurate monthly management account statements (P\&L, Balance Sheet and Cashflow) for the overall business. * Manage month end schedule to ensure time frames are understood by all other functions and their relevant contributions are provided on time. * Work closely with the Financial Controller to ensure that month end is completed to an excellent standard in a timely manner. * Monthly analysis of overheads, liaising with budget holders \& reporting variances to senior management including commentary for board packs * Review and approval of manual journals for trainee accountant * Manage and control of key balance sheet accounts monthly and quarterly across multiple divisions, including the reconciliation of intercompany transactions and corporation tax. * Co\-ordination the completion of balance sheet reconciliations across finance teams. * Manage the Group Reporting and consolidation process * Responsible for annual consolidated financial statements * Review and approve of payroll files/bank reconciliations/creditor reconciliations monthly. * Review and approval of bi\-monthly payment runs on the bank including the setup of new beneficiaries. * Take a lead role in the annual external Audit for all companies. * Manage and minimise financial and control risk. * Play a key role with the improvement of SAP ERP System and provide process training to other team members to ensure correct processes are followed. * Actively participate in on\-going finance related IT developments, collaborating in a cross functional team. Be an innovative thinker with a willingness to suggest new ideas to complement and enhance existing processes. * Develop and implement optimised and standardised routines, tools, and processes to drive performance within the finance function. * Lead and develop junior members of the team, offering guidance and support. * Support the Financial Controller and VP of Global Finance to meet the business needs, as required. **Experience and Qualifications:** * ACA/ACCA/CIMA Qualified Accountant with a minimum of 3 years PQE * Exceptional communication skills, strong focus on customer service both internal and external, self starter with ability to embrace change and process improvement * Experience in a high growth environment preferably in a PE backed fast\-moving\-consumer\-goods (FMCG) * Experience reporting into senior management. * Demonstrated curiosity and a passion for continuous learning. * Excellent organisation and time management skills with the ability to manage several priorities at once. * Exceptional communication and presentation skills with a strong desire to succeed. * Excellent attention to detail and has experience working to tight deadlines. * Excellent inter\-personal skills with ability to develop \& nurture relationships across the entire business. * Continuously strive to improve how we operate through streamlining processes or eliminating inefficiencies. * Proven record of achievement, with specific examples of process improvement achievements * Experience in large ERP system an advantage * Advanced Excel \& PowerPoint skills a significant advantage * An analytical proactive thinker who can delve into detail to capture, summarise and present key issues For further information, please contact Orla Brennan 086 1720090 / orla.brennan@prismexecutive.ie

title: HSE Advisor
description: **Building a sustainable tomorrow** Working at BAM means delivering projects that make a difference. From the major infrastructure that keeps society moving, to the social housing, schools, and community spaces that shape daily life. We don’t just talk about social value. We embed it into every build. We are recruiting a **Project Health,Safety \& Environmental Advisor** to join our Carlow based project. The role of the Project HSE Advisor is to guide, support and advise Project Managers and other relevant personnel on the setting up, implementation and continuous maintenance of HSE systems and standards on site in accordance with BAM Safety and Environmental Management Systems. **Making Possible** * Promote safe working practices and ensure the implementation of company HSE policy and procedures on site. * Carry out regular site HSE inspections and report back findings to relevant personal. * Ensure that the project has been provided with the relevant HSE documentation. * Ensure that HSE project plans meet company standards. * Ensure that HSE issues are discussed at project review meetings. * Keep and maintain clear records to comply with company internal and external audit standards. * Deliver HSE training where appropriate and as agreed with the Project Manager. * Keep up\-to\-date on Health, Safety and Environmental legislation. * Communicate Demonstrate and Promote BAM HSE ethos, values and concepts. * Become a Role Model for Safe Behaviour. * Carry out other relevant duties as required from time to time. * Liaising with the relevant legal authorities (i.e. H.S.A, EPA etc.) * Knowledge of all legal requirements especially in regard to the PSCS role and duties. * Ensure all accidents and dangerous incidents are thoroughly investigated and reports provided to the HSE \& * Sustainability Manager detailing causes, conclusions and recommendations to avoid recurrence. * Provide the HSE Department with relevant HSE information on a quarterly basis. **What’s in it for you?** Opportunities! Opportunities to work with a great team on some of Irelands largest, dynamic and most exciting construction and civil engineering projects. Opportunity to develop your skills to the highest industry standards at one of the country's largest construction firms. We are committed to our employees and offer strong competitive salaries along with benefits such as: * 8% pension contribution. * Private Health Insurance. * Dental Plan. * 26 days annual leave, incremental with years service. * 2 Wellbeing days annually. * Paid maternity \& paternity leave. * Learning \& Development opportunities \- internal progression is encouraged and fostered. * Company vehicles (role dependent). * .....and many more great perks. **What do you bring to the role?** * Level 7 qualification in Health and Safety. * 3 years successful postgraduate experience in Project HSE Management. * Thorough knowledge of ISO: 45001 and ISO: 14001 standards. * Working towards graduate membership of IOSH. * Ability to deliver internal Health and Safety management courses. * Ability to advise on HSE issues on a day to day basis on site. * Ability to conduct standard accident/incident investigations. * Ability to write HSE site reports. * Qualification in Environmental Management is desirable. **About BAM** Building a sustainable tomorrow. That’s our mission and our promise at BAM. It’s how we engineer vital infrastructure and construct high\-quality buildings as one of the largest construction companies in Europe. We strive to create an environment where everybody feels welcome and valued. We’re on an exciting journey to employ the best talent to join us regardless of social background, race, colour, religion, national or ethnic origin, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, disability or other characteristics. **The application process** BAM is committed to ensuring a fully inclusive recruitment and onboarding process, so if at any time you feel we need to do something to make it more accessible to you, do not hesitate to let us know. Click the link to apply and Join us in making possible \#ie1 * Please note, CV's will not be accepted directly by email.

title: Human Resources Business Partner
description: **HR Business Partner (12\-Month Fixed\-Term Contract – Maternity Cover)** **Location: Dublin 2 (Hybrid after training period)** A prestigious and well\-established real estate organisation based in Dublin 2 is seeking an experienced **HR Business Partner** to join their team on a 12\-month fixed\-term contract to cover maternity leave. This is a key **operational HR role** , suited to someone who enjoys being hands\-on and involved in the day\-to\-day running of the HR function, while supporting senior stakeholders across the business. **The Role:** Reporting directly to the CEO, the HR Business Partner will be responsible for delivering a comprehensive and efficient HR service across the organisation. This role will focus heavily on the operational side of HR, ensuring smooth execution of all core HR processes and acting as the go\-to person for HR\-related matters. **Key Responsibilities:** * Manage the day\-to\-day HR operations across the business * Act as the first point of contact for all HR queries from employees and management * Handle employee relations matters including disciplinaries, grievances, and performance management * Process and manage payroll, ensuring accuracy and compliance * Oversee recruitment, onboarding, and offboarding processes * Maintain HR systems, employee records, and reporting * Ensure full compliance with Irish employment legislation and internal policies * Support and manage one direct report * Drive efficiency and continuous improvement across HR processes **Requirements:** * Proven experience in an HR Business Partner or senior HR Generalist role with a strong operational focus * Hands\-on experience managing payroll * Strong knowledge of Irish employment law and HR best practice * Comfortable working in a standalone or lean HR environment * Experience managing or mentoring a junior team member * Highly organised with strong attention to detail * CIPD qualification or equivalent is desirable **What’s on Offer:** * Competitive salary of €70,000 – €80,000 * Benefits package * Hybrid working (office\-based initially during onboarding/training) * Direct exposure to senior leadership * Opportunity to take ownership of a broad and varied HR function **To Apply:** To apply for this position, please reach out to **Richard Minchin** on the Lex Consultancy team for a confidential discussion.

title: IT Helpdesk Engineer
company: MCS Group | Your Specialist Recruitment Consultancy
description: **Job Title:** IT Support Engineer **Location:** Dublin (On\-site) **Salary:** Up to €45,000 \+ bonus \+ many other amazing benefits About the Company We're working with a long\-standing Dublin\-based business that has built a strong reputation over the years and continues to grow. They're a team of 250\+ people, working on large, high\-profile projects with some really well\-known global clients. It's a busy, fast\-moving environment, but one that genuinely puts a lot of emphasis on culture, collaboration, and keeping things enjoyable day to day. From a tech perspective, they've invested well and continue to evolve, so you're not stepping into something stale or overly rigid. The Opportunity This is the kind of IT Support role people often say they want, but don't always find. Yes, there are tickets. But this isn't a "sit behind a screen and close tickets all day" type job. You'll be out on the floor, speaking to people, solving problems in real time, and becoming a go\-to person across the business. The role naturally gives you a lot of visibility, and that's something the team really values. You'll be joining a small IT team where everyone gets stuck in. There's a good mix of day\-to\-day support and exposure to wider systems and projects, so you're constantly learning rather than just repeating the same tasks. It's a great move for someone who feels like they've outgrown a standard service desk role and wants something a bit more hands\-on and engaging. What You'll Be Doing You'll be supporting the wider business with whatever comes up day to day \- from user issues and device setups to getting involved in broader fixes and improvements. Some days will be busier than others (roughly 15\-30 tickets between the team), but the key difference here is how the team works together. It's collaborative, you're not left on your own, and there's a shared responsibility to get things sorted quickly. A big part of the role is also how you interact with people. You'll be dealing with everyone from junior staff to senior leaders, so being able to explain things clearly and build trust is just as important as your technical ability. What They're Looking For * Around 3\-5 years' experience in IT Support * Someone comfortable working in a fast\-paced environment * Solid fundamentals across Microsoft technologies and general troubleshooting * Most importantly, someone who enjoys working with people and being visible in the business They're not overly focused on certifications. If you want to pursue them, they'll support you, but attitude and approach matter more here. How This Role Works This is an on\-site role, and that's intentional. Because of how hands\-on and people\-facing the role is, being in the office is a big part of it. There is flexibility to work from home one day a week once you've settled in, but day\-to\-day you'll be in the mix with the wider team. What's on Offer Salary is up to €45,000, alongside a solid benefits package including bonus, pension, learning support, and a genuinely social working environment. It's the kind of place where people know each other, get involved, and enjoy coming into work \- which makes a big difference in a role like this. If you're in that 3\-5 year range and want a role where you're learning, visible, and actually part of the business rather than just supporting it from the sidelines, this is well worth a conversation. How to Apply To apply for this role, submit your CV via the application form provided. Alternatively, to speak in absolute confidence about this opportunity, please contact Jack Tyrrell via j.tyrrell@mcsgroup.jobs Disclaimer If you have a disability which means you require assistance at any stage of the recruitment process, please contact us directly to discuss. We are committed to providing equality of opportunity to all. Please note, we are receiving an exceptionally high number of applications at present and will be unable to shortlist candidates who are not meeting the specific requirements for this role. Due to the high volume of applications, we may be unable to provide individual feedback. We thank you in advance for your understanding.

Engaged with innovative initiatives, your expertise will propel our business into the future. Collaborating with a creative team of tech enthusiasts, you’ll contribute your unique skills to fuel our technological advancements. **What You’ll Do as** **ITSM Process Owner \- Incident \& Problem Management** In your role, you’ll collaborate with different people across a range of skillsets. Here’s a flavour of your day\-to\-day: * Own the end\-to\-end governance, consistency, and continual improvement of one or more ITIL process domains across Primark’s technology estate, ensuring alignment with business needs, ITSM strategy, and compliance across internal teams and third‑party partners, reporting to the Service Assurance Lead. * Act as the accountable owner for assigned ITIL processes (e.g., Incident, Problem, Change Management), defining, documenting, and maintaining policies, standards, workflows, and procedures while ensuring consistent adoption, governance, and compliance against agreed KPIs and SLAs. * Serve as the subject matter expert and escalation point for process\-related issues, supporting integration with ITSM tools such as ServiceNow, identifying optimisation and automation opportunities, and collaborating with Service Desk, Service Owners, Transition Managers, Product Teams, and Engineering to ensure operational supportability. * Define, maintain, and analyse process metrics and KPIs, lead performance and compliance reviews, and deliver regular dashboards and reports to the Service Assurance Lead and stakeholders, highlighting trends, risks, insights, and improvement opportunities. * Identify, prioritise, and implement Continual Service Improvement initiatives to improve maturity, efficiency, and service quality; facilitate improvement workshops and reviews; track progress and outcomes, ensuring alignment with broader ITSM and organisational objectives. * Engage and collaborate with internal service teams, product and engineering functions, and third‑party providers to drive process adherence and shared accountability, promote awareness of process value and behaviours, and support training and change communication initiatives. **What You’ll Get** People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle: Healthcare, pension, and potential bonus. 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Bring** Here at Primark, we want everyone to feel valued – so please bring your authentic self to work, of course with some other key experience and abilities for this role in particular: * 4\+ years of experience in IT Service Management with proven responsibility for managing or owning ITIL processes, supported by a strong understanding of ITIL v4 (or v3\) in enterprise and multi‑supplier environments. * Demonstrated experience implementing, governing, and improving ITSM processes across internal teams and external providers, including managed service providers and SIAM or multi‑vendor operating models. * Proficiency in ITSM platforms such as ServiceNow and associated reporting tools, with the ability to monitor performance, compliance, and improvement opportunities. * Strong analytical and problem‑solving capability, using data‑driven insights to support decision‑making, process optimisation, and continual improvement initiatives. * Excellent communication, collaboration, and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to influence process adoption, compliance, and behavioural change across diverse teams. * Desirable experience including ITIL v4 Managing Professional or Intermediate certification, retail or distributed business environments, leading CSI or process maturity assessments, and familiarity with Agile or DevOps ways of working and their integration with ITSM processes. Does this sound like you? Great, because we can’t wait to see what you’ll bring. You’ll be supported within a team of equally capable people, celebrating who you are and aiding you reach your potential. At Primark, we’re excited about our future \- and we’re excited to develop yours. **About Primark** At Primark, people matter. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together. If you need any reasonable adjustments or have an accessibility request, during your recruitment journey, such as extended time or breaks between online assessments, a sign language interpreter, mobility access, or assistive technology please contact your talent acquisition specialist. All offers of employment are subject to background checks, including right to work, reference education and for some roles criminal, and financial checks. If you have any concerns, please reach out to our talent acquisition team to discuss. **REQ ID:** JR\-8231

title: In-House Lawyer
description: **In\-House Lawyer, Virgin Media Ireland** *(Senior Legal Counsel, 12 Month Fixed Term Contract)* **About Virgin Media:** Virgin Media Ireland is home to the country’s fastest broadband and is the leading provider of connected entertainment and broadcasting. We power millions of connections nationwide every single day, delivering award‑winning ultrafast broadband, TV, mobile and home phone services that keep Ireland working, streaming and communicating at superfast speed. Our Virgin Media Business division provides the fastest broadband speeds and advanced connectivity solutions for entrepreneurs, SMEs, large organisations and the public sector. Virgin Media Television is Ireland’s number one commercial public service broadcaster, delivering four free‑to‑air channels \- Virgin Media One, Virgin Media Two, Virgin Media Three and Virgin Media Four, along with Virgin Media Play, our state‑of‑the‑art free streaming service. We deliver fresh, diverse and original Irish content \- from news and current affairs to entertainment, factual and drama \- alongside the best of international programming. Virgin Media Ireland is part of Liberty Global Ltd. (Nasdaq: LBTYA, LBTYB, LBTYK), a leading international converged connectivity and investment company. Liberty Global manages three complementary platforms: Liberty Telecom, which provides around 80 million fixed and mobile connections across Europe and generates $21\.6 billion in aggregate revenue; Liberty Growth, which invests in scalable technology, media, sports and infrastructure businesses valued at $3\.4 billion; and Liberty Services, which delivers technology, operational and financial services generating approximately $700 million annually\*. Together, these platforms create long‑term, sustainable value for shareholders. For more information, visit www.virginmedia.ie. **About the Role:** This is a great opportunity for a 5 years PQE solicitor to join the legal team at Virgin Media. Virgin Media operates in a complex, highly competitive and regulated telecoms and broadcasting market in which a broad range of issues impact on strategic and business development. We’re looking for someone with a strong sense of commercial awareness and ability to understand and analyse technical business concepts. The successful candidate will have excellent interpersonal skills and a creative approach to problem solving. We are seeking a skilled commercial lawyer to join our dynamic in\-house legal team on a 12 month fixed term contract basis. Reporting to the Legal Director, primary responsibilities will involve providing pragmatic legal advice and drafting support across a broad range of commercial matters within a fast\-paced telecommunications environment. **Specific Duties \& Tasks:** * Accountable for complex commercial legal matters of significant strategic and/or financial value, identifying risk, escalating as appropriate, recommending mitigation and implementing solutions. * Negotiation of commercial contracts including supplier arrangements, contracts for software, hardware and middleware, interconnect supply and technical delivery and dealings with outsource partner(s) and other material suppliers. * Partner closely with key commercial and operational stakeholders to provide proactive legal advice and support on business growth initiatives, including new products, services, partnerships and strategic projects, ensuring legal risk is managed while enabling timely and commercially sound decision‑making. * Lead all legal work for the Wholesale and Business Enterprise teams, including negotiation of customer contracts, assessment of legal risk and delivery of practical, solutions‑focused advice. * Provide commercial and regulatory legal advice across the business on marketing, advertising, customer communications and consumer protection. **Skills \& Expertise:** * 5 years Post Qualified Experience * Experienced lawyer with proven track record of diverse significant experience in commercial transactions and demonstrable legal expertise, gained in private practice and/or in\-house * Excellent commercial judgment and provision of practical solutions * Experience advising telecommunications companies which was gained in private practice and/or in\- house an advantage * Strong knowledge of the Irish legal and regulatory framework applicable to telecommunications businesses, with the ability to analyse its impact on commercial arrangements and provide clear, pragmatic legal advice to the business. * Solid negotiation, technical and drafting skills, with an exceptional ability to attend to detail * Strong commercial acumen and an interest in working across all areas in the business * Experience working in a fast\-paced organisation, managing multiple priorities and ensuring deadlines are consistently met * An ability to balance the need to use initiative while working collaboratively as part of a team **The Person:** * The ability to communicate effectively and efficiently with the business while ascertaining the key impact/detail behind the issue/request. * A ‘team player’ who can lead by example and inspire others on a daily basis. * Ability to work in a fast\-paced environment and embrace change. * High attention to detail, someone who cares about quality in everything they do. * A methodical approach to both daily tasks and project work. * Explores opportunities by being resourceful and self\-motivating. * A first class problem solver. Virgin Media is an equal opportunities employer. Having a diverse workforce is critical to the success of our business.

title: Internship Officer (Studio+) – Creative Futures Academy / NCAD (0.5 FTE)
description: **Post Title \& Subject Area** **Internship Officer (Studio\+) Creative Futures Academy / NCAD (0\.5 FTE)** **Grade:** Grade V **Post Duration:** 15\-month specified purpose funded contract **Reporting Relationship:** Reports to: Head of Department \- Innovation \& Engagement **Salary:** Grade V salary scale 52,240 62,482 per annum pro\-rata (26,120 31,241\) (Standard public sector terms and entry at minimum point apply) **Location:** NCAD, 100 Thomas Street, Dublin 8 **Hours of Work:** 17\.5 hours per week (net of breaks) **Annual Leave:** 25 days pro\-rata (12\.5 days), exclusive of public holidays **Role Overview:** NCAD seeks to appoint an Internship Officer to support the delivery, coordination, and continuous enhancement of the Studio\+ internship and real\-world learning model within the Creative Futures Academy. This role is central to enabling high\-quality, practice\-based learning experiences that connect students with the creative industries. It combines industry engagement, academic coordination, student support, and operational delivery. The postholder will be responsible for developing and sustaining a pipeline of relevant internship opportunities; ensuring alignment between placements and academic learning outcomes; coordinating assessment processes; and capturing the impact of internships through structured case studies and reporting. The role requires a candidate who can operate effectively across academic and professional contexts, with strong organisational capability, stakeholder management skills, and an understanding of creative sector practices. **Key Responsibilities:** **1\. Internship Development and Industry Engagement** * Source, develop, and maintain a diverse pipeline of high\-quality internship opportunities across the creative and cultural sectors. \[DMcG1] * Build and sustain relationships with industry partners, public bodies, and cultural organisations. * Act as a key liaison between NCAD and external stakeholders, ensuring clear communication of expectations, processes, and mutual value. **2\. Academic Alignment and Quality Assurance** * Work closely with academic staff to ensure internships align with programme learning outcomes and assessment frameworks. * Support the development and implementation of learning agreements and assessment criteria. * Monitor the quality of internship experiences and address issues in a timely and structured manner. **3\. Student Engagement and Support** * Guide students in sourcing, selecting, and preparing for internships appropriate to their discipline and career ambitions. * Deliver briefings, workshops, and induction sessions on internship processes, expectations, and professional practice. * Support students in setting learning objectives and engaging in reflective practice throughout their placement. * Provide ongoing support during internships, including managing challenges and referring students to appropriate supports where necessary. **4\. Assessment and Module Coordination** * Coordinate the delivery of internship\-related assessment processes in collaboration with academic staff. * Support the preparation and dissemination of assignment briefs aligned with module learning outcomes. * Manage and maintain internship modules on Canvas (or equivalent LMS), including submission points, communications, and tracking of student engagement. * Oversee the collection, organisation, and collation of student submissions for academic review and assessment. **5\. Operational Delivery and Administration** * Manage end\-to\-end internship processes, including opportunity promotion, approvals, documentation, and reporting. * Ensure all placements are supported by appropriate documentation (e.g. learning agreements, memoranda of understanding where required). * Maintain accurate and auditable records of internships, student participation, and partner engagement. * Ensure compliance with institutional and regulatory requirements, including health and safety, insurance, and data protection. **6\. Systems Development and Continuous Improvement** * Develop and maintain clear, scalable workflows, guidance materials, and documentation for students, staff, and partners. * Contribute to the standardisation and continuous improvement of internship processes across Studio\+ programmes. * Gather and analyse feedback from students, staff, and partners to enhance the effectiveness and efficiency of delivery. * Support reporting requirements related to programme evaluation, accreditation, and strategic planning. **7\. Case Study Development and Impact Reporting** * Develop structured internship case studies that capture the full lifecycle and impact of internship experiences. * Synthesise information from internship job descriptions, student outputs (including learning logs and reflective journals) (anonymised), and employer feedback. * Produce clear, high\-quality summaries that articulate: * + the nature of the placement and student contribution + the skills and competencies developed + the value of the experience to both student and host organisation * Contribute to the use of these case studies as strategic assets for: * + student recruitment and programme promotion + industry engagement and partnership development + evidencing the impact of Studio\+ within the creative sector **Core Competencies** The successful candidate will demonstrate: * Strong understanding of the creative industries and professional practice contexts * Proven ability to build and manage external partnerships * Experience coordinating complex, multi\-stakeholder programmes or processes * Ability to ensure quality and consistency in work\-based learning environments * Strong organisational and administrative capability, with attention to detail * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills * A student\-centred approach with a focus on employability and professional development * Capacity to develop structured processes and improve systems over time * Proficiency in digital platforms, particularly learning management systems (e.g. Canvas) **Selection Criteria** **Essential** * A minimum of: * + (a) 3 years relevant experience in a similar role **and** a relevant qualification (NFQ Level 6 or higher), **or** + (b) 5 years relevant professional experience * Demonstrable experience working with external stakeholders or partners * Strong organisational, coordination, and administrative skills * Experience supporting programme delivery, training, or work\-based learning initiatives * Excellent written and verbal communication skills * Ability to work independently and collaboratively across teams **Desirable** * Experience in a higher education environment, particularly in art, design, or creative disciplines * Experience coordinating internships, placements, or experiential learning modules * Experience developing industry\-academic partnerships or live project briefs * Experience producing case studies, reports, or impact\-focused documentation * Familiarity with Canvas or similar LMS platforms * Understanding of graduate employability and practice\-based education models **Summary** This role is critical to the successful delivery and ongoing development of Studio\+ at NCAD. It requires a balance of operational efficiency, academic coordination, industry engagement, and strategic insight. The Internship Officer will play a key role in ensuring that internship experiences are high\-quality, well\-structured, and impactful, while also capturing and communicating their value to support the continued growth and reputation of NCADs practice\-based learning model. **Closing Date: 20th of May 2026**

title: Investment Operations Specialist - 12 Month Contract
description: **Company:** ------------ Mercer **Description:** ---------------- Mercer’s Investment Management business, Investment Solutions International (IS), is looking for a motivated and detail\-oriented investment operations specialist to join our Operations team. **This is a 12 month fixed term contract role with potential for permanency subject to performance and business needs** . While prior experience in regulatory reporting or fund expense oversight is a plus, it is not required. We are looking for someone with a basic understanding of fund accounting and a keen interest in learning about European investment products and operational processes. **What can you expect:** Mercer is a fast\-growing investment solutions provider with a broad and expanding client base. Our fund range includes approximately 140 Irish domiciled funds across multiple legal structures, supporting Mercer’s investment and risk management solutions for institutional clients. The Operations team supports a variety of important activities including onboarding new clients, launching funds, managing fund rebalancing, overseeing NAV and valuations, and supporting regulatory and tax compliance. You will work closely with experienced colleagues and third\-party service providers to help ensure smooth and accurate operations. The Regulatory Reporting team focuses on ensuring Mercer meets its regulatory obligations, including reporting under MiFID II, AIFMD, MMFR, EMIR, and PRIIPs KIDs. As an Associate, you will become responsible for reporting processes and gain exposure to regulatory changes and process improvement. In addition, you will support Fund Expenses Oversight liaising closely with our Dublin lead and Mumbai team to ensure all fund expense related tasks are completed accurately and on time. This role offers a great learning environment where you will support \& contribute to key operational and regulatory tasks, develop your knowledge of investment operations, and actively work on team projects. **We will rely on you to:** * Learn and develop a solid understanding of Mercer’s fund structures and investment solutions * Support the team in delivering accurate and timely regulatory reporting and fund expense oversight * Act as primary subject matter expert and support for offshore colleagues. * Assist with data validation, monitoring, and basic analysis to ensure data quality * Help research and summarize regulatory requirements and fund operations under guidance * Support process improvements and automation initiatives to enhance operational efficiency, driving forward solutions and taking ownership of outcomes. * Collaborate with internal teams and external service providers to support daily operations * Contribute to team projects and support the implementation of new regulatory requirements * Maintain a strong focus on risk management and compliance within your work **What you will need to have:** * 2\-5 years of experience in asset management, fund administration, or investment operations, with relevant academic background (Business, Accounting, Finance, Economics). * Good understanding of fund accounting principles and investment products. * Strong attention to detail and willingness to learn. * Excellent analytical skills and comfort working with data (Excel proficiency required; SQL or other data tools a plus). * Interest in regulatory frameworks and compliance processes (prior experience is a bonus but not essential). * Effective communication skills and ability to work collaboratively in a team environment. * Self\-motivated, self\-starter with a positive attitude and eagerness to develop professionally. * Ability to manage multiple tasks and work in a fast\-paced environment. * High performance outlook comfortable taking ownership of work and driving solutions. * Good technology skills including Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Word) and openness to learning new tools. **What will make you stand out:** * Enthusiasm for building a career in investment operations and regulatory reporting * Proactive approach to learning and problem\-solving * Strong organizational skills and ability to deliver work accurately and on time * Excellent verbal and written communication skills * Ability to quickly absorb new information and adapt to change * Confident and comfortable in making decisions. **Why join our team:** * To be part of a company with a strong brand, committed to making a difference in people's lives. * We help you be your best through professional development opportunities, interesting work and supportive leaders. * We foster a vibrant and inclusive culture where you can work with talented colleagues to create new solutions and have impact for colleagues, clients and communities. * Our scale enables us to provide a range of career opportunities, as well as benefits and rewards to enhance your well\-being. \#MercerIS Mercer is a business of Marsh (NYSE: MRSH), a global leader in risk, reinsurance and capital, people and investments, and management consulting, advising clients in 130 countries. With annual revenue of over $27 billion and more than 95,000 colleagues, Marsh helps build the confidence to thrive through the power of perspective. For more information about Mercer, visit mercer.com, or follow us on LinkedIn and X. ### **Marsh is committed to embracing a diverse, inclusive and flexible work environment. We aim to attract and retain the best people and embrace diversity of age, background, disability, ethnic origin, family duties, gender orientation or expression, marital status, nationality, parental status, personal or social status, political affiliation, race, religion and beliefs, sex/gender, sexual orientation or expression, skin color, Traveller community, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. We are an equal opportunities employer. We are committed to providing reasonable support to any candidate with a disability/health condition to allow them to fully participate in the recruitment process. We welcome candidates to contact us at reasonableaccommodations@mmc.com to discuss any specific needs.** ### **Marsh is committed to hybrid work, which includes the flexibility of working remotely and the collaboration, connections and professional development benefits of working together in the office. All Marsh colleagues are expected to be in their local office or working onsite with clients at least three days per week. Office\-based teams will identify at least one “anchor day” per week on which their full team will be together in person.**

title: Java Developer
description: **Company Description** Version 1 has celebrated 30 years in business and continues to be trusted by global brands to deliver technology and transformation solutions that drive customer success. Our deep expertise enables our customers to navigate the rapidly evolving technology landscape. We foster strong partnerships with global technology leaders including Microsoft, AWS, Oracle, Red Hat, OutSystems, Snowflake, ensuring that our customers are provided with the highest quality solutions and services. We’re an award\-winning employer reflecting how our employees are at the very heart of what we do: * UK \& Ireland's premier AWS, Microsoft \& Oracle partner * 3300\+ strong, €350/£300m revenue business * 10\+ years as a Great Place to Work in Ireland \& UK * Best Workplace for Women in the UK \& Ireland by GPTW * Best Workplace for Wellbeing in the UK by GPTW We’re a core valuesdriven company, we hire people who share our values, and we reward those who display and foster them, it’s deeply embedded within our DNA. Invest in us and we’ll invest in you. **Job Description** Job Description: We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Java Developer to join our dynamic team. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in Java development, extensive experience with AWS, database management, and preferred work experience in batch processing. As a Java Developer, you will play a crucial role in designing, developing, and maintaining high\-performance applications that meet our business needs. Key Responsibilities: * Design and Development: Design and development of robust, scalable, and efficient Java applications. * AWS Integration: Utilize AWS services to build and deploy cloud\-based solutions, ensuring high availability and scalability. * Database Management: Design, implement, and maintain database schemas, write complex SQL queries, and optimize database performance. * Batch Processing: Develop and manage batch processing systems to handle large volumes of data efficiently. * Code Quality: Ensure code quality through code reviews, unit testing, and adherence to best practices. * Collaboration: Work closely with cross\-functional teams, including product managers, QA engineers, and other developers, to deliver high\-quality software solutions. * Troubleshooting: Identify and resolve performance bottlenecks, bugs, and other technical issues. * Mentorship: Provide guidance and mentorship to junior developers, fostering a culture of continuous learning and improvement. **Qualifications** * Education: Bachelor’s or master’s degree in computer science, Engineering, or a related field. * Experience: Proven experience of 5\+ years working as a Java Developer in product development or services environment. * AWS Skills: Minimum 3\+ years of work experience in AWS services such as EC2, S3, Lambda function, Step\-functions, Event bus etc. * Database Skills: Minimum 5\+ years of work experience with relational databases (e.g., Oracle, MySQL, PostgreSQL), ability to write complex joins, performance troubleshooting experience etc. * Technical Skills: Proficiency in Java, Spring Framework, Hibernate, and RESTful APIs. * Problem\-Solving: Excellent analytical and problem\-solving skills. * Communication: Strong verbal and written communication skills. * Team Player: Ability to work effectively in a collaborative team environment. Preferred Qualifications: * Batch Processing experience: Hands\-on experience with batch processing frameworks and tools. * Python experience: Minimum of 2 years is nice to have. **Additional Information** **Why Version 1?** At Version 1, we believe in providing our employees with a comprehensive benefits package that prioritises their wellbeing, professional growth, and financial stability. * Share in our success with our Quarterly Performance\-Related Profit Share Scheme, where employees collectively benefit from a share of our company's profits * Strong Career Progression \& mentorship coaching through our Strength in Balance \& Leadership schemes with a dedicated quarterly Pathways Career Development programme * Flexible/remote working, Version 1 is tremendously understanding of life events and people’s individual circumstances and offer flexibility to help achieve a healthy work life balance * Financial Wellbeing initiatives including; Pension, Private Healthcare Cover, Life Assurance, Financial advice and an Employee Discount scheme * Employee Wellbeing schemes including Gym Discounts, Bike to Work, Fitness classes, Mindfulness Workshops, Employee Assistance Programme and much more. Generous holiday allowance, enhanced maternity/paternity leave, marriage/civil partnership leave and special leave policies * Educational assistance, incentivised certifications, and accreditations, including AWS, Microsoft, Oracle, and Red Hat * Reward schemes including Version 1’s Annual Excellence Awards \& ‘Call\-Out’ platform. * Environment, Social and Community First initiatives allow you to get involved in local fundraising and development opportunities as part of fostering our diversity, inclusion and belonging schemes. And many more exciting benefits… drop us a note to find out more. Version 1 is an equal opportunities employer. We are committed to building a diverse, inclusive and respectful workplace where everyone feels valued and able to thrive. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities and lived experiences, and we value the different perspectives people bring. We want every candidate to have a positive and accessible recruitment experience. If you need reasonable adjustments at any stage of the process, please contact ekta.bahl@version1\.com at Version 1\. We will consider all requests carefully, respectfully and confidentially. Video links: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v\=F\_d3ELTH5zo \#LI\-EB1

title: Job Specification for Communications and Engagement Intern  (paid internship)
description: **Overview** Codema, Dublin’s Energy Agency, is currently offering a very exciting six\-month paid internship across its Public and Stakeholder Engagement and Communications and Marketing teams. This internship provides a great opportunity for students or recent graduates in the area of Climate Change, Communications or a similar discipline to gain industry experience and apply what they have learnt in college. The intern will benefit from mentoring by Codema’s experienced staff while developing practical skills and gaining professional experience in one of Ireland’s most respected and leading not\-for\-profit organisations within the energy sector. The successful candidate will get the opportunity to work across several innovative projects and events, such as local community engagement on Decarbonising Zones, EU\-funded community\-focused projects as well as helping to support the organisation and roll\-out of public information events, demos, workshops and conferences. We are committed to leading Dublin's low\-carbon transition and we are looking for someone who is equally committed to these values and realises the key role that effective communication and local engagement plays in achieving this. Codema is a leading energy agency in Ireland, so this is a unique opportunity to gain experience and be at the forefront of driving awareness on the need to move Dublin away from fossil fuels through these various engagement activities and events. **What you will do** You will support the delivery of core communications and engagement outputs across both our Public and Stakeholder Engagement and Communications and Marketing teams. The role will involve tasks and opportunities to learn in areas such as community and stakeholder engagement, event planning, and communications and digital marketing. **Key tasks and responsibilities \- public and stakeholder engagement** ● On\-the\-ground/in\-person engagement with local communities and stakeholders. ○ Responsibilities include stakeholder mapping, preparing an engagement plan and tracking engagements, supporting the running of a local energy advice clinic. ● Support in the organisation of local engagement events and workshops. ○ Responsibilities include supporting Codema’s Public Engagement and Stakeholder Coordinator in designing workshop plans, organising venue and catering, supporting with facilitation, note\-taking and transcribing. ● Support in development and design of communications and engagement materials for local engagement projects ● Support in the integration of impact measurement tools to support engagement activities. ○ Responsibilities include research into suitable impact measurement tools and supporting the trial of relevant tools at engagement activities. ● Support in the creation of a detailed database of potential funding streams to support PASE. **Key tasks and responsibilities \- communications and marketing** ● Event coordination and delivery support ○ Support the Communications and Marketing team to plan, promote and deliver events, including conferences, workshops, EU project events and webinars. Responsibilities include managing registrations, liaising with speakers and sponsors, coordinating logistics and event materials, supporting promotion and stakeholder outreach, and helping ensure events run smoothly online and on\-site. ● Support Codema’s Home Energy Saving Kit and Think Energy campaign activities, including: ○ Providing event support to Codema’s Energy and Climate Awareness Officer, as needed, including at public information stands and Home Energy Saving Kit demonstrations ○ Providing administrative support for the Home Energy Saving Kit scheme, including responding to customer queries, fulfilling orders and managing stock ● Provide digital marketing support across Codema and Think Energy channels, including website, social media platforms and e\-newsletters. This will involve: ○ Creating engaging and creative content ○ Posting regularly across social media accounts ○ Posting news articles and updating website content ○ Assisting in the development of regular e\-newsletter ● Promotional material ○ Support the team with content creation and the design of promotional materials, including brochures, reports, information leaflets and infographics. **We are looking for** ● A team player who has excellent organisational and communication skills ● An eager learner who can hit the ground running ● Someone with a growth\-mindset who will grow and learn from working on real projects ● Someone who is not afraid to ask for help when they need it and will be always supported by the Codema team ● A people\-person with a real ‘can\-do’ attitude ● Outgoing and lots of positive energy **Minimum requirements** ● Currently studying or recently graduated with a Masters or Bachelor’s Degree in Climate Change/Marketing/Communications or similar discipline ● Proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel and PowerPoint ● Good communication skills with the ability to communicate effectively ● Excellent organisational and time management skills ● Fluency in English is essential ● Ability to work with a team and on own initiative ● Eligible to work in Ireland and available to start in June/July 2026 **Additional skills that would be a distinct advantage** ● Interest in the energy and climate landscape in Ireland ● Knowledge and experience of website Content Management Systems (e.g. Word Press, etc.) and social media platforms ● Experience with event organisation ● Knowledge and experience of design platforms such as Canva ● Experience working with a range of stakeholders **Location** The Codema office is located in Dublin city and is accessible by a range of sustainable transport modes. Secure bicycle parking is also available. The internship will be a mixture of remote and in\-office working, with in\-person work required for all events. **Duration** Six\-month full\-time paid internship, commencing June/July 2026\. **Salary** €2,469/month **Next steps** Please e\-mail your CV and a short cover letter to recruitment@codema.ie by 5pm, Friday, 15th May 2026\. As we may be advertising several positions, please insert the title of the position you are applying for in the subject line of your email. We aim to hold interviews the week of 25th May 2026\. **About Codema** We are Codema — Dublin’s Energy Agency. We are driven by our vision to positively impact people’s lives locally, while addressing the environmental damage caused by climate change at a global scale. We believe we can do this by improving the way we produce and use energy locally, while benefiting the people of Dublin. For more information, please visit our website at www.codema.ie. **Codema is an Equal Opportunities Employer**

title: Junior Accounts Payable Administrator
description: **Are you looking to become part of a collaborative team whilst maximising your potential to grow and develop within your career? Apply today.** **The Role.** GMC Utilities Group Ltd are seeking an experienced and detail\-oriented **Junior** **Accounts Payable Administrator** to join our growing Finance Team, based in Ballycoolin, Dublin 11\. This is a key role within the organisation, supporting the Finance Department in managing subcontractor accounts payable processes, ensuring accuracy, efficiency, and compliance in all financial transactions. **Key Duties and Responsibilities will include but are not limited to:** **Key Responsibilities:** Upload weekly subcontractor costs into Sage, creating purchase order cost accruals. Process large volumes of subcontractor invoices against accrued costs, deducting any necessary retentions. Maintain accurate subcontractor creditor balances at all times. Ensure all agreed credit terms are correctly recorded and utilised on the accounting system. Complete creditor statement reconciliations on a regular basis. Prepare weekly and monthly payment lists using aged creditor listings and notes on Sage 200 for review and approval. Notify payments through ROS and process any required RCT deductions within Sage. Review and adjust accrued costs to ensure accuracy. Handle supplier and subcontractor queries promptly and professionally. **The Candidate: Required Experience and Competencies:** Proven experience in an Accounts Payable or Finance Administration role. Strong organisational skills and attention to detail. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to collaborate across departments. Flexible approach and ability to manage variable workloads. Strong IT skills, particularly Microsoft Excel (advanced level) and Sage 200 experience (desirable). Fluent English, both written and verbal. **Become a part of something bigger.** At the heart of GMC is our people, from engineers through to customer service and we aim to work collaboratively towards our common goals. GMC is an equal opportunity employer, and we aim to recruit and retain like\-minded people from diverse backgrounds with a wide range of expertise. In GMC we foster a work culture that promotes collaboration and learning, building an environment where our people have the capacity to maximise their potential whilst gaining the opportunity to continuously develop. **Our Future.** Since the founding of GMC in 1981 by Gerry McCloskey, GMC has grown to be Ireland's leading Civils and Utilities company and the future for GMC is bright. We have engaged with our staff and other stakeholders to agree and publish our core values. One of our core values is to *"Focus on the Future"* . Our growth to date reflects the confidence that our customers have in our service delivery, in the innovative approach that we bring to how we do our work, and to our culture of safety and quality. We have ambitious plans to develop our footprint in Ireland and overseas but understand that every decision we make contributes to people's quality of life throughout generations, and we don't take that responsibility lightly as we build Ireland's infrastructure of tomorrow, today.

title: Junior Accounts Payable Administrator
description: **Are you looking to become part of a collaborative team whilst maximising your potential to grow and develop within your career? Apply today.** **The Role.** GMC Utilities Group Ltd are seeking an experienced and detail\-oriented **Junior** **Accounts Payable Administrator** to join our growing Finance Team, based in Ballycoolin, Dublin 11\. This is a key role within the organisation, supporting the Finance Department in managing subcontractor accounts payable processes, ensuring accuracy, efficiency, and compliance in all financial transactions. **Key Duties and Responsibilities will include but are not limited to:** **Key Responsibilities:** Upload weekly subcontractor costs into Sage, creating purchase order cost accruals. Process large volumes of subcontractor invoices against accrued costs, deducting any necessary retentions. Maintain accurate subcontractor creditor balances at all times. Ensure all agreed credit terms are correctly recorded and utilised on the accounting system. Complete creditor statement reconciliations on a regular basis. Prepare weekly and monthly payment lists using aged creditor listings and notes on Sage 200 for review and approval. Notify payments through ROS and process any required RCT deductions within Sage. Review and adjust accrued costs to ensure accuracy. Handle supplier and subcontractor queries promptly and professionally. **The Candidate: Required Experience and Competencies:** Proven experience in an Accounts Payable or Finance Administration role. Strong organisational skills and attention to detail. Excellent communication and interpersonal skills with the ability to collaborate across departments. Flexible approach and ability to manage variable workloads. Strong IT skills, particularly Microsoft Excel (advanced level) and Sage 200 experience (desirable). Fluent English, both written and verbal. **Become a part of something bigger.** At the heart of GMC is our people, from engineers through to customer service and we aim to work collaboratively towards our common goals. GMC is an equal opportunity employer, and we aim to recruit and retain like\-minded people from diverse backgrounds with a wide range of expertise. In GMC we foster a work culture that promotes collaboration and learning, building an environment where our people have the capacity to maximise their potential whilst gaining the opportunity to continuously develop. **Our Future.** Since the founding of GMC in 1981 by Gerry McCloskey, GMC has grown to be Ireland's leading Civils and Utilities company and the future for GMC is bright. We have engaged with our staff and other stakeholders to agree and publish our core values. One of our core values is to *"Focus on the Future"*. Our growth to date reflects the confidence that our customers have in our service delivery, in the innovative approach that we bring to how we do our work, and to our culture of safety and quality. We have ambitious plans to develop our footprint in Ireland and overseas but understand that every decision we make contributes to people's quality of life throughout generations, and we don't take that responsibility lightly as we build Ireland's infrastructure of tomorrow, today.

title: Junior Business Analyst
description: **Junior Business Analyst (AI)** **Initial 3\-6 month contract** **Dublin, Hybrid** **My client a leading professional services company is on the lookout for a Junior Business Analyst to join their team.** The Job: * Support the development lifecycle of software projects by acting as a bridge between technical teams and business stakeholders. * Gather and document requirements, ensuring they are translated into clear, actionable user stories and functional specifications. * Assist in the integration of AI features, helping to define use cases, data requirements, and success metrics for machine learning models or automated workflows. * Conduct gap analysis and process mapping to identify opportunities for software optimization and AI\-driven efficiency. * Facilitate UAT (User Acceptance Testing) by creating test scripts and ensuring the final product aligns with the original business intent. * Maintain project documentation and backlogs within Agile environments. Have You: * Proven 1\-2 years experience as a Junior Business Analyst, specifically within a software development or IT environment. * Hands\-on exposure to AI or Data Science projects, with an understanding of how AI can be applied to solve business problems. * Strong requirement elicitation skills, including the ability to run workshops and interview stakeholders effectively. * Experience working in Agile/Scrum frameworks. * Analytical mindset with the ability to interpret complex data and present findings to non\-technical audiences. * Immediate availability to commit to an initial 3\-6 month contract period. Candidates must have a stamp 4 visa, UK/EU Passport\* **Berkley Business \& Technology is a specialist consultancy, recruiting professionals for organizations throughout Ireland, UK \& Europe; for more information go to www.berkley\-group.com.** **If you would like to discuss this vacancy further or to discuss your career options in confidence, please send your profile to brendanoconnell@berkley\-group.com**

title: Junior Field IT Support
description: **Job Summary** **JOB DESCRIPTION – Junior Field IT Support** Location:Dublin (Onsite) Division: Ticketmaster UK/IRE – Enterprise Support Contract Terms: Permanent, 40 hours per week **THE TEAM** The Technology Support team which is part of the Global Support \& Operations organisation, is a customer focused service delivery team providing technical support and installations services across the various business units and client base. We are a key department responsible for the successful delivery of major events and festivals as well as ensuring the ongoing support and maintenance of our fixed client base and owned and operated venues. Focused on providing high levels of customer and client satisfaction we are driven to provide safe, secure and reliable solutions and services that enables fans to enjoy the experience of live events. **THE JOB** The Technology Support team will focus their day to day by providing support to our clients and the wider Ticketmaster and Livenation business either remotely or in person. Supporting a wide range of products, peripherals and Ticketmaster proprietary software you will need to be technically focused and customer centric. The Technical Support team will often work in the pressurised but enjoyable environment of festivals and outdoor events installing a wide range of hardware and software solutions that provides fans with a seamless and enjoyable experience attending Live events. **What You Will Be Trained To Support** * Installation and support of Ticketmaster proprietary software and hardware e.g. Ticket printers, Handheld Devices and Point of Sale * Installation of software, firmware to ensure devices are up to date * Installing and supporting network devices including Wireless Access Points, Switches, Bridges etc * Upgrading and replacing hardware and software as and when required * Configuring and testing of the above said hardware and software * Diagnosing and remediation of issues and problems both remotely and on\-site * Raising incidents and updating incident management software * Tracking Assets and updating asset management software * Minimise incidents and service distribution by providing proactive support and maintenance * Coordinating and liaising with clients prior to attendance and installations * Working with third party providers and contractors often in a lead capacity * Provide on\-site end user training and demonstration of Ticketmaster products * Updating and maintaining technical and operational documentation * Communicating and collaborating with multiple teams and clients * Following best practices and procedures ensuring standards are maintained * Maintain an understanding of all TM and LN products usage and functionality **What You Need To Know** * A willingness or keen interest or knowledge of Microsoft, and mobile device applications. * An interest in learning wired and wireless networking technologies and products * Eager to expand knowledge within the entertainment business and its requirements to meet our client’s needs, be committed to your development within the Junior support role. **YOU** * An aptitude for understanding concepts and methodologies, both technical and non\-technical * Good interpersonal and communication skills – Fluent English essential * Candidate must be detail oriented and organised * Ability to travel required * May be required to work nights, weekends, or holidays * Must have valid clean driving licence * Candidate must be dynamic to meet the needs of an ever\-changing live entertainment environment **TICKETMASTER VALUES** * Reliability – We understand that fans and clients rely on us to power their live event experiences, and we rely on each other to make it happen. * Teamwork \- – We believe individual achievement pales in comparison to the level of success that can be achieved by a team * Integrity – We are committed to the highest moral and ethical standards on behalf of the countless partners and stakeholders we represent * Belonging \- We are committed to building a culture in which all people can be their authentic selves, have an equal voice and opportunities to thrive **EQUAL OPPORTUNITIES** We are passionate and committed to our people and go beyond the rhetoric of diversity and inclusion. You will be working in an inclusive environment and be encouraged to bring your whole self to work. We will do all that we can to help you successfully balance your work and home life. As a growing business we will encourage you to develop your professional and personal aspirations, enjoy new experiences, and learn from the talented people you will be working with. It’s talent that matters to us and we encourage applications from people irrespective of their gender, race, sexual orientation, religion, age, disability status or caring responsibilities. **CULTURE** We’re fans who help fans everywhere get in to the live events they love. A proud division of Live Nation Entertainment, Ticketmaster has changed the way the world connects with their favourite artists, teams, and shows, and we continue to shape innovation every day. We’re not just selling tickets (though we do that better than anyone else), we’re enriching lives one amazing experience at a time. And we think that’s pretty amazing. If you’re passionate about the magic of live entertainment like we are, and you want to work at a company dedicated to helping millions of fans experience it, we want to hear from you. We have a ‘Fan First’ mindset, placing customers at the heart of everything we do. Whether working behind the scenes or engaging directly with the ‘Fan’ you will be part of a culture that supports and enriches the fan experience. Your work, our passion… delivering world class experiences – Fan First. Live Nation Entertainment will never request payment or equipment purchases as part of the hiring process. Recruiters will only contact candidates from official Live Nation or affiliated brand email domains.
