title: Junior Pensions Consultant
description: **Junior Pensions Consultant** **About Your New Employer** Join a leading provider of pensions solutions and be part of an innovative and dynamic organisation focused on employee engagement and investment offerings. As Junior Pensions Consultant you will have the opportunity to work within a team dedicated to supporting thousands across the construction industry with pension, life assurance, and illness benefits. **About Your New Job** * As Junior Pensions Consultant you will deliver engaging presentations (in\-person and virtual) to pension scheme members across multiple schemes. * You will explain pension benefits, options, and retirement processes in a clear, accessible way and help increase member understanding. * You will support member engagement campaigns, respond to follow\-up queries, and promote Additional Voluntary Contributions (AVCs). * As Junior Pensions Consultant you will tailor your communications for different audiences (e.g., site staff, pre\-retirees), maintaining high levels of accuracy and clarity, and collaborate with internal teams to ensure consistency and support the development of communication materials. **What Skills You Need** * QFA qualified * Strong presentation and public speaking skills, with the ability to explain complex topics simply. * Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities, confident with both large and small groups. * Organised and goal\-oriented, able to manage your own diary and work to deadlines. * Relevant experience in pensions, financial services, or employee benefits (preferred). **What's on Offer** The chance to make a real impact by enhancing financial literacy and future readiness for members in a critical sector. Excellent professional development opportunities in a supportive and collaborative environment. Activity target driven role with a focus on engagement and education, not sales or regulated advice. **What's Next** Apply now by submitting your application or contact Aoife Lowe, 01 474 4643, alowe@sigmar.ie. to take your next career step. We welcome candidates seeking a rewarding and meaningful role in a unique, supportive environment.

title: Junior Recruitment Consultant - No Experience Required
description: * **Transparent promotion pathway — you control how fast you progress** * **Uncapped commission — your results determine your income** * **Industry\-leading training — equips you with elite\-level expertise** At Archer, advancement is earned. We run a performance\-led environment with clearly defined goals, open expectations, and progression driven by results, not tenure or office politics. Strong performance doesn’t go unnoticed here; it’s recognised quickly and rewarded with accelerated career growth. High performers earn 3 promotions in their first year! We specialise in developing ambitious professionals into Recruitment and Business Development experts. You’ll benefit from practical, one\-on\-one coaching, a clear and structured progression framework, and a commission structure with no ceiling, expanding as you progress. Our consultants build genuine market expertise, unlock significant earning potential, and move forward in their careers far faster than in most fields. **Why Join the Team?** * **Drive Growth:** Meet clients across the IT market and help power our expansion with outstanding service and results. * **Make an Impact:** Play a key role in shaping our success while fast\-tracking your own career. * **Level Up Fast:** Get expert training and mentorship to grow into a top performer and future leader. * **Earn Without Limits:** Benefit from a strong base salary plus uncapped commission that truly rewards high achievers. **Your day\-to\-day as a Recruitment Consultant:** * **Client Management:** Build and maintain strong relationships with clients from a varied range of industries. * **Candidate Sourcing:** Proactively source, screen, and qualify candidates for various roles within the IT sector. Utilise a variety of sourcing methods, including LinkedIn, networking, and database management. * **Market Insight:** Stay informed about the IT industry, including trends, challenges, and opportunities. Use this knowledge to inform recruitment strategies and provide insights to clients and candidates. **What You Will Have:** * 3\+ years of professional experience (any industry can be considered) \- No recruitment experience required * A proactive, ambitious mindset and the confidence to take ownership of your growth * Strong communication skills and a natural drive to succeed **Benefits \& Perks** * Basic Salary \+ Uncapped Commission \+Additional Bonus Structure (Average OTE: Year 1 €48k\+, Year 2 €69k\+, Year 3 €100k\+) * Internal Learning and development, helping support and train alongside the support of your manager * Clear path for progression: 13 promotions available all the way to Equity Partner \+ Leadership Academy programme for managers in the making * Sabbatical: Paid sabbatical every 3 years * Industry\-leading training programme with 150\+ training modules * Fantastic team culture, very collaborative and regular team socials **Turn ambition into reward** At Archer, performance drives growth and uncapped earnings. Apply today and start building the career you deserve. Contact Ciara O’Flynn at **ciara.oflynn@archer.ie** or **\+353 1 902 6804\.**

title: Junior Sales Representative - Trade Counter Retail
description: **About Us:** Established in 2002, Concrete Care has become a leading specialist in the sale and hire of a diverse range of surface preparation and dust extraction equipment. Our extensive product lineup includes concrete floor grinders and polishers, industrial buffers and burnishers, scarifiers and shavers, floor scrapers, shot blasters, industrial vacuums, forced action mixers, power floats, handheld power tools, electric mini dumpers, and more. We pride ourselves on delivering top\-notch customer service and maintaining a great team environment. **Job Description:** We are seeking a dynamic and customer\-focused Sales Advisor to join our showroom team in Ballyfermot, Dublin. The ideal candidate will play a crucial role in driving sales, providing exceptional customer service, and ensuring smooth operations within the showroom. **Please note** **that this position cannot consider overseas applications.** **Responsibilities:** * Assist customers in the showroom with product inquiries and sales, offering knowledgeable and professional advice. * Make outbound calls to potential and existing customers to drive sales and provide product information. * Handle customer orders over the phone and via email efficiently and accurately. * Place orders with suppliers and manage incoming stock deliveries. * Maintain stock levels in the showroom, ensuring products are displayed attractively and restocked as needed. * Support the showroom team with inventory management and stock\-taking activities. * Provide excellent customer service, addressing customer queries and resolving issues promptly. * Utilise computer systems for order processing, inventory management, customer communications, and CRM software. * Collaborate with the team to achieve sales targets and contribute to a positive work environment. **Requirements:** * Proficiency in computer skills, including experience with inventory management, sales, and CRM software. * Excellent communication skills in English, both written and verbal. * Strong customer service orientation with a friendly and professional demeanour. * Ability to work independently and as part of a team. * Previous experience in a tool hire shop or counter sales is preferred. * Good organisational skills and attention to detail. * Reliable and punctual with a strong work ethic. **Benefits:** * Competitive salary and potential for commission. * On\-site parking available. * Supportive and friendly team environment. * Opportunity to work with a leading company in the concrete preparation and flooring industry. If you are passionate about sales and customer service and have an interest in the construction and flooring industry, we would love to hear from you. To apply, please submit your CV and cover letter detailing your relevant experience and why you are a perfect fit for this role. Concrete Care is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €30,000\.00\-€35,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Employee discount * On\-site parking * Sick pay * Store discount Work Location: In person

title: Junior Sous Chef/Sous Chef
description: Reporting to the Head Chef and Senior Sous Chef We are a long\-established kitchen with a strong reputation for consistency, quality and professionalism. With a highly experienced head chef and sous chef who have built and maintain standards over many years, this is an environment rooted in discipline, teamwork and pride in craft. This is an opportunity to step into a kitchen where systems are proven, expectations are clear and development is taken seriously. As a junior sous chef, you will support the senior kitchen leadership in the day to day running of the kitchen. Maintaining standards, leading sections and ensuring service runs smoothly. This role is ideal for a very strong senior chef de partie or current sous chef ready to step up and learn with a structured and high performing team. Key Responsibilities; Assist head chef and sous chef in managing all kitchen operations Take ownership of sections during prep and service Ensure food quality, consistency and presentation meet established standards Maintain levels of cleanliness and food safety compliance Contribute to stock control, ordering and waste management Step into leadership during service when required What we are looking for; Previous experience as strong chef de partie or junior sous chef Solid understanding of kitchen operations A willingness to learn Strong work ethic and ability to perform under pressure Attention to detail and pride in your own work Good communication and team leadership skills Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €38,000\.00\-€40,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

title: Junior Structural Engineer
description: **Shape a future with purpose at Arup in Cork.** Arup’s purpose, shared values and collaborative approach has set us apart for over 75 years, guiding how we shape a better world. Our Building Engineering team sits at the heart of Arup's commitment to sustainable development. We engineer innovative solutions that minimize environmental impact and create resilient, sustainable structures. **The Opportunity** Join our Building Engineering team in Cork and contribute to impactful projects across Ireland and Europe. This role provides an excellent opportunity for career development and the chance to work on cutting\-edge structural engineering projects. **As a Structural Engineer At Arup, You Will** * Undertake structural analysis using GSA, Tekla Structural Designer, or other structural software. * Complete the design of structural elements in reinforced concrete, steel, timber, or masonry. * Prepare mark\-ups and work with structural technicians to produce structural drawings using Revit. * Represent Arup at meetings with the client, other consultants and/or contractors. * Conduct site visits and structural investigations. * Prepare reports, presentations, and site reports. * Collaborate with the Design Team on Common Data Environments such as ACC. **Does this role inspire you?** We are seeking a highly motivated and technically proficient individual with a passion for structural engineering and a commitment to sustainable design. **Candidates Must Have** * 1 \- 3 years of post \- graduate experience in structural design. * Degree qualified in Civil/Structural Engineering or similar. MEng or equivalent preferred . **We Also Value** * Excellent communication and report writing skills, including the ability to communicate technical concepts clearly and effectively. * Proficiency in relevant design software packages . * Interest in using and developing digital design tools to deliver smart solutions. **Why Arup?** At Arup, we believe in investing in our people and supporting their growth from day one. As a trust\-owned company, we're driven by our purpose and values, creating a collaborative and rewarding environment for our employees. **Benefits That Work For You** * Professional Growth \& Development: Benefit from continuous learning opportunities, and receive guidance from experienced mentors. * Global Opportunities: Collaborate with colleagues worldwide and explore potential short\-term or long\-term assignments at other Arup offices and project locations. * Financial Well\-being \& Ownership: Competitive benefits package and share in Arup's success through our global profit\-sharing program. * Wellbeing and Culture: At Arup we are committed to fostering a healthy working culture by offering a range of health and wellbeing benefits, including health insurance, flexible working schemes, wellness programmes and an Employee Assistance Programme. * Equality, Diversity and Inclusion: We strive to embed inclusion in our way of working, our interactions with one another and the projects we deliver. We are committed to creating an inclusive working environment for talented people of any background, based on merit, fairness and respect. At Arup, you belong to an extraordinary collective where individuality is encouraged. Our strength comes from how we respect, share, and connect our diverse experiences, perspectives, and ideas. You'll have the opportunity to do socially useful work that has meaning – to Arup, to your career, and to the clients and communities we serve. We welcome applications from talented individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of age (within legal limits), gender identity or expression, disability, ethnicity, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected characteristic. We are committed to making our recruitment process and workplaces accessible. \#IREGrad **Do you want to join us in shaping a better world?**

title: Kubernetes Engineer
description: **Overview** We are seeking a Kubernetes Engineer to join our global infrastructure team and help scale, automate, and secure our container orchestration environments across on\-premises and public cloud platforms. As a Kubernetes specialist, you’ll work closely with DevOps, SRE, and security teams to deliver reliable, self\-service, and production\-ready Kubernetes clusters that power mission\-critical applications. **Key Responsibilities** **Cluster Management** * Deploy, manage, and upgrade Kubernetes clusters using tools like kubeadm, EKS, AKS, GKE, or Rancher. * Implement robust RBAC, network policies, ingress controllers, and security frameworks in Kubernetes. **Automation \& IaC** * Automate cluster provisioning and application deployment pipelines using Terraform, Helm, and ArgoCD. * Build reusable modules for consistent infrastructure delivery across staging and production. **CI/CD Integration** * Integrate Kubernetes with modern CI/CD workflows for rapid, safe application delivery. * Support GitOps practices and continuous deployment automation. **Monitoring, Logging \& Troubleshooting** * Implement observability for Kubernetes using Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, and Fluentd/Fluent Bit. * Troubleshoot performance issues, failed pods, memory leaks, and cluster degradation events. **Cloud \& Hybrid Deployments** * Operate Kubernetes workloads across AWS, Azure, GCP, and hybrid/on\-prem environments. * Use tools like Velero, Kasten, or Stash for backup/restore strategies in Kubernetes. **Collaboration \& Support** * Partner with application developers, SREs, and security teams to implement best practices. * Serve as a technical advisor on cloud\-native architectures and containerization s **Experience/Qualification Required** * Certified Kubernetes Administrator (CKA) – Highly preferred * Certified Kubernetes Application Developer (CKAD) * Certified Kubernetes Security Specialist (CKS) * AWS Certified DevOps Engineer – Professional * Google Professional Cloud DevOps Engineer * HashiCorp Certified: Terraform Associate * Linux Foundation Certified System Administrator (LFCS) **Nice to have:** * Experience with GitOps tools (ArgoCD, Flux). * Observability tools like Prometheus, Grafana, ELK/EFK stack. * Experience with service mesh technologies (Istio, Linkerd). * Open\-source contributions in Kubernetes\-related projects * Spectro cloud, Incus, Cloud 9 etc… **About InterSystems** InterSystems, a creative data technology provider, delivers a unified foundation for next\-generation applications for healthcare, finance, manufacturing, and supply chain customers in more than 80 countries. Our data platforms solve interoperability, speed, and scalability problems for large organizations around the globe to unlock the power of data and allow people to perceive data in imaginative ways. Established in 1978, InterSystems is committed to excellence through its 24×7 support for customers and partners around the world. Privately held and headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts, InterSystems has 38 offices in 28 countries worldwide. For more information, please visit **InterSystems.com** .

title: Kurdish Sorani speaker - Trust & Safety, Analyst
description: **€73,000 • Full‑time • Onsite in Dublin • Trust \& Safety / Global Response Org • 11 FTC** **Deadline:** 01/05 \- 11am We’re looking for a **Kurdish Sorani \+ Arabic‑speaking Data Labelling Analyst** to join the Global Response Org for one of the world’s biggest tech companies. This is a **high‑impact role** where you’ll help keep online communities safe, understand emerging risks, and support real‑time investigations across the platform. If you speak **Kurdish Sorani** fluently (Iraq/Iran region) and you’re already based in **Ireland** , this could be a fantastic opportunity. **What you’ll be doing** * Review and analyse content and escalations in Sorani \+ Arabic * Identify risks, trends, and violations across organic content, ads, and commerce * Act as the “market expert” for the Sorani‑speaking region (Iraq/Iran) * Use internal AI tools to support investigations and improve model accuracy * Work closely with policy, legal, engineering, and vendor teams * Support crisis response, elections, and sensitive real‑time issues * Help improve processes, tools, and operational workflows * Work onsite in Dublin as part of a global 24/7 team (occasional on‑call) **What we’re looking for** * **Fluency in Kurdish Sorani \+ Arabic** (essential) * **5\+ years in Trust \& Safety, Online Operations, or similar** * Strong understanding of Sorani‑speaking markets (Iraq/Iran) — cultural, political, linguistic nuance * Experience reviewing sensitive or high‑risk content * Ability to think critically, spot patterns, and propose solutions * Comfortable working with graphic/objectionable material * Strong communication skills and ability to collaborate across teams * Basic analytical skills (Excel, SQL, reporting tools) **Location** This role is **fully onsite in Dublin** . You must already be in Ireland or willing to relocate here. If you speak **Kurdish Sorani** , have Trust \& Safety experience, and want a role where your work genuinely matters, I’d love to hear from you. Call on \+353 15225356 or send me a message to iraia.sanchez@recruiters.ie ***If you are curious to know more and if your profile fits perfectly with this position, please reach out to Iraia at \+353 15225356 or send us your updated CV to iraia.sanchez@recruiters.ie*** ***By applying here you agree to RECRUITERS storing your CV in our secure applicant tracking system (ATS; Bullhorn). title: Lawyer
description: **Job Title:** Lawyer **Department:** Legal **Contract Type:** Permanent **Location:** Dublin, Ireland (Hybrid) **Why work with us?** We are the bank for a changing world and we aim to build positive change together: * We are a team of colleagues who are enthusiastic about what we do and the impact our work has on our clients. We work in a highly collaborative, open\-minded environment that embraces innovation and creativity * We offer competitive remuneration and a comprehensive benefits package, including a discretionary bonus scheme, company pension contribution, comprehensive private healthcare packages, health and fitness subsidy, work\-from\-home subsidy, above statutory days of annual leave and numerous other benefits. * We are strongly committed to wellbeing and work\-life balance, including a 50% hybrid work model (where the role allows), paid family leave, fertility and menopause support — available to support all life stage needs. * Equality, diversity and inclusion are at the core of our culture, and we actively promote equal opportunities through creating or being part of initiatives and programmes: * Aspire Female Leadership Programme, provides development and exposure to high\-potential women in the bank, through targeted workshops, executive coaching and formal mentoring * Proud members of 100 Women in Finance and among the first signatories of Ireland’s Women in Finance charter * Joined the \#JamaisSansElles (Never Without Her) Charter, committing its executive committee to only join public or media events with panels of three or more speakers, if at least one of those speakers is a woman * Development and mobility are actively encouraged. BNP Paribas’ culture is built on purposeful talent development and recognition programmes that foster engagement, inclusive leadership and continuous growth. A few of our programmes include: * Leaders for Tomorrow, a programme that fast\-tracks high\-potential talent through training, development and cross\-functional projects, preparing them for future leadership positions * Local and Global Mentoring initiatives, that pair employees with senior leaders, providing staff with platforms to gain advice on career growth topics * Ambassador Awards employee recognition scheme provides rewards to those who have embodied the Bank’s core values * Local Experts (Champions) Programme, identifying internal subject\-matter experts from specific business areas, who excel at their craft * We are a global company operating in over 60 countries with over 180,000 employees from over 150 nationalities. In Ireland we have approximately 550 employees across Dublin and Galway, working in Corporate Institutional Banking (CIB) and BNP Paribas Security Services (BP2S) and Greenval **Equality and Diversity** BNP Paribas is an equal opportunities employer and proudly cultivates a diverse workforce. We believe that diversity of experience enhances our service offering to clients and leads to a better working environment. We welcome applications from the best candidates regardless of age, ethnicity, gender, gender identity, nationality, disability, sexual orientation, socio\-economic background, parental and caring status, or religious belief. Our Employee Resource Groups (ERGs) play a key role in promoting diversity and inclusion through initiatives, events and advocacy programmes that foster a sense of belonging for all colleagues. In BNP Paribas Ireland, we have dedicated networks for parents and caregivers, LGBTQA\+, staff from diverse cultural and religious backgrounds, alongside forums to discuss CSR and gender related topics. **Sustainable Finance** BNP continues to demonstrate the Bank’s steadfast commitment to supporting its clients in their growth ambitions by expanding market reach across assets, delivering excellence in bond markets, and showing the way in sustainable financing. **Role** The Dublin Legal team (CIB), comprises the Head of Legal and 6 other lawyers, a company secretary and support staff. It serves a broad spread of CIB businesses with a strong focus on supply chain management and Transaction Banking (including corporate lending and flow banking products such as Cash Management). We require an Irish or English qualified lawyer on a full\-time basis to join the legal team to support the growing business lines and various functions in the territory. **Key Responsibilities** * Participation in the Financing Legal Platform, including project and transactional support as required. * Support the Supply Chain Management (“SCM”) Group through each of the SCM teams around the world, particularly Dublin, Paris and London, in documenting their transactions and developing robust structures. * Working on the Payable and Receivable structures including analyses of novel electronic platform solutions for the GTS Supply Chain Team. * Working on the implementation of any Bank led projects * Monitoring any developments in legislation and identification of legal risk in the territory and reporting into the SARA tool. * Answering ad hoc queries on NDA’s or any Account opening or onboardings. * Assisting Compliance with sanctions papering and or escalations. * Advising on day to day general corporate law matters, primarily corporate governance queries, arising from the Bank’s activity and the Bank’s subsidiaries activities. * Queries on deposit and current accounts and any new investment and loan products, including marketing materials, if required. * Contributing to IP/IT legal matters that may be allocated to you. * Liaising with business colleagues within the global banking network in the structuring, drafting, negotiation and closing of transactions for the Bank. * Supporting commercial banking activities including bilateral and syndicated lending and trade services offerings. * Working closely with the global trade services team from a legal perspective, including the drafting and review of supply chain finance transactions, working with third party platforms, risk participations, review and drafting of bank guarantees, stand\-by letters of credit etc. * Dealing with queries on account opening, mandates, deposit and current accounts and any new investment and loan products. * Advising on relevant regulatory requirements relating to the Bank’s business and its customers. * Provision of legal advice in relation to corporate governance and the overall legal interests of the Bank. * Leading or participating in various projects being run by the Dublin Legal team or as part of the global Financing Legal Platform. * Keeping up to date on current topics affecting the Bank such as ESG, data protection, PSPs, Accelerated Payment products etc. * Drafting various types of security documents including account charges, guarantees etc. including any filings or perfection steps. * Instructing external legal counsel on certain matters falling outside of the day to day scope of the Dublin Legal team. **Miscellaneous** * Formulation and review of legal policies: identifying initiatives for improvements in the implementation of the legal work of the Bank, with emphasis on efficiency and legal risk management and operational control. * Monitoring any developments in legislation and identification of legal risk in the territory. * Assisting with the co\-ordination of the Regulatory Watch Group framework for all Irish BNP Paribas entities. * Assisting with the Bank’s pro bono initiatives in EMEA as well as local pro bono projects. * Preparation of training materials and delivery of training to staff across the Bank. * Assisting the Head of Legal with reporting requirements. **Business Skills** **Qualifications \& Experience required for this role** * Risk analysis * Compliance knowledge * Banking law * Civil and commercial / contract law * Legal Knowledge * Business Organisation **Transversal Skills** * Analytical ability * Ability to develop and leverage networks * Ability to conduct a negotiation * Ability to understand, explain and support change **Behavioural Skills** * Client Focus * Ability to collaborate/Teamwork * Ability to synthetise/simplify * Creativity \& Innovation Problem Solving **Other Skills** * Excellent communication skills; * Strong attention to detail; * Strong team working ethic; * Ability to work in a fast paced environment with multiple conflicting deadlines; * To work in an efficient and timely manner, with an emphasis on attention to detail. * Robust yet approachable and collegial nature, with the ability to act calmly and professionally in a demanding and fast moving business environment; * Independent, thorough and commercially minded, and have strong time management and prioritising skills; * Strong legal knowledge, specifically banking, civil and commercial / contract law; * Strong risk analysis and compliance knowledge; * Comfortable and confident advising the business in a demanding environment; * Creativity and innovative problem solving skills; * Ability to understand, explain and support change; * Competent with MS PowerPoint, Excel and MS Word; **About BNP Paribas** At BNP Paribas, we center our values on what drives the company forward. Our driving forces are: * Agility: we want to behave more simply to embrace useful innovation and digital transformation * Client Satisfaction: we believe that our success lies in being the customers’ and clients’ preferred choice. We seek to listen carefully to them and work closely with them * Compliance Culture: We believe in promoting clear rules to foster a strong culture of compliance and ethics * Openness: We promote open\-minded attitudes towards our stakeholders. We aim for everyone in Group to feel included, to have their say and be empowered **Our Strengths Are Based On Four Key Areas** * Stability: we build upon our solid, long\-term oriented management, our diversified and integrated business model and our international footprint * Expertise: We build upon our recognized and expanding knowledge of our teams * Responsibility: We build upon our culture of responsibility and integrity to ever better serve the interest of our customers * Good Place to Work: We foster a stimulating workplace where people are treated fairly and with respect **Management excellence** BNP continues to foster top\-class people management practices within our strong people focused culture. BNP Paribas managers follow the Group’s Management Charter, which emphasises a positive, empowering team culture, encouraging open communication and supporting development and wellbeing. **Compliance Culture** We believe in promoting clear rules to foster a strong culture of compliance and ethics. We would expect any incoming member of staff to respect all financial security related legislation, including anti\-money laundering (AML); countering the financing of terrorism (CFT); international financial sanctions and embargoes (IFS); anti\-bribery and corruption (ABC); KYC; ongoing client related data screening; and transaction monitoring. BNP Paribas have clear policies on these topics which all staff are expected to familiarise themselves with upon successful entry to the Group. Should you require any reasonable accommodation for the interview process, please let us know. By submitting your application, you agree to BNP Paribas completing a background check and screening in advance of any potential offer being made.

title: Legal Counsel Europe IB
description: **Overview** PepsiCo's Law and Compliance \& Ethics function helps drive business objectives and sustainable results while protecting PepsiCo—legally, ethically, and reputationally. We align with the business, with dedicated lawyers and legal professionals supporting each Sector and BU/market, alongside Global Group/Corporate specialists covering practice areas and governance needs. We work Faster, Stronger, Better by leveraging technology, simplifying processes, and expanding capabilities—while advancing diversity and inclusion within our department and the broader legal profession. By joining the award winning Legal team supporting International Beverages Europe (IB Europe) as Legal Counsel – Europe IB (Franchise), you will be an integral member of the Europe Legal team, providing legal support for our Franchise (FOBO) beverage markets in Europe (excluding Russia), and assisting the General Counsel across all FOBO territories. **Role Purpose** Provide proactive, business centric legal counsel across Europe FOBO markets—acting as a strategic partner to franchise leadership, safeguarding PepsiCo through robust governance, practical risk management, and high quality contracting—while contributing to Europe wide initiatives and standards. **Reporting line:** Reports to the General Counsel, Europe IB **Responsibilities** * Strategic business partnering: Drive timely, high quality legal support for franchise operations; proactively analyze and mitigate risk while enabling growth. * Primary legal point of contact: Serve franchise leaders (Franchise Directors/Managers) and Finance/Control teams as first line independent counsel on day to day matters. * Litigation strategy \& management: Under the General Counsel's supervision, oversee external counsel, set litigation strategy, and implement mitigation plans. * Franchise governance \& transactions: Draft, negotiate, and manage core franchise agreements and amendments, including Bottling Appointments, Concentrate \& Marketing Agreements, and Business Development Agreements. * Commercial contracting: Issue accurate, reliable contracts across franchise, control, supply chain, sales, and finance—aligned to PepsiCo policies, precedents, and controls. * Risk \& corporate governance: Identify and manage legal risk/exposure with third parties; ensure robust corporate governance and complete corporate records. * Policy implementation \& enablement: Implement company policies and provide cross functional support on the documentation needed for effective execution. * Digital \& process excellence: Simplify/automate legal processes; drive the continued roll out of digital tools across Europe IB. * Training \& capability building: Design and deliver targeted training (e.g., anti bribery/anti corruption, antitrust/competition, key contract provisions); uplift legal acumen across client groups. * Collaboration across IB system: Partner with Europe OU Functions, IB Legal, Global Law Department, PPGA, and Trademark teams to apply global precedents and best practices. **Qualifications** **Minimum Qualifications:** * LL.B. (Bachelor of Laws) required; LL.M. is an advantage. * Qualified lawyer, admitted in country of qualification, with minimum 8 years' experience (law firm and/or in house). * FCPA and EU Competition Law: required. * GDPR/data privacy: knowledge is a plus (and familiarity with evolving European privacy frameworks). * Demonstrated experience in corporate compliance, drafting/reviewing/negotiating a broad range of commercial contracts, and stakeholder management across complex, multi market environments. * Fluency in English (spoken \& written); fluency in other EU languages is an advantage. * Ability to prioritize multiple matters to meet business needs; strong computer literacy, negotiation, and communication skills. * Professional, mature, and effective under pressure in fast paced environments; comfortable partnering with senior and junior colleagues. * Team player with a collaborative mindset. **Preferred Skills:** * Strong attention to detail, analytical rigor, and resourcefulness; significant autonomy in decision making with limited supervision. * Self starter who leads projects and delivers on time with clear stakeholder engagement. * Demonstrated leadership—able to work independently and within a collaborative team. * High learning agility—quickly understands local law/regulatory developments and PepsiCo's business; learns from external best practices and cascades knowledge across the team. * Excellent interpersonal, project management, time management, and organizational skills. * Excellent command of computer skills, negotiation and communication skills. * Professional, mature, able to work under pressure in a fast\-paced environment. * Team player who is flexible and confident working with both senior and junior colleagues.

title: Legal Counsel – Multinational Tech Company
description: My client is looking for an experienced Legal Counsel to join their team in Dublin. They are a major Multinational company growing their team in Ireland at scale. You’ll provide legal advice to management on strategic initiatives, business operations, regulatory matters, and compliance in Ireland. Lead corporate legal activities and ensure timely, effective legal support across the business, including drafting, reviewing, and negotiating contracts. **Key Responsibilities** * Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of commercial contracts and legal documents. * Advise on contractual, statutory, and regulatory obligations, ensuring ongoing compliance in customer engagements. * Conduct legal due diligence and research to support business decisions. * Provide legal counsel across corporate, commercial, and risk management matters. * Identify and mitigate legal and operational risks. * Interpret statutes, regulations, and contracts, and advise on resolving legal and business issues. * Partner with internal stakeholders to deliver clear, practical legal guidance. * Manage and support legal projects and, where required, supervise junior legal staff. * Report regularly to the Head of Legal on key matters and developments. **Skills \& Experience** * Qualified lawyer (LLB/LLM/JD or equivalent) with a current practising certificate. * 5–10 years’ experience, including at least 5 years in outsourcing, IT contracts, or licensing. * Strong drafting, negotiation, and analytical skills, with solid knowledge of relevant laws. * Effective communicator with the ability to work across all levels and cultures. * Strong organisational, project management, and prioritisation skills. * Collaborative, proactive, and able to operate independently with minimal support. * Experience managing or mentoring team members is an advantage. * Willingness to travel domestically and internationally as required. Salary dependent on candidate experience. Benefits: Annual Bonus Scheme. Contributory Pension. Private Medical Insurance. Life Assurance \& Long\-Term Disability. Employee Assistance Programme. 22 days annual leave \+ 10 public holidays. Continuous Learning \& Development. Access to extensive training \& certification resources. Lunch \& Learn sessions. Additional perks including company discounts, on\-site parking, and bike\-to\-work scheme **Based in Dublin, Ireland. Hybrid.** Candidates must be eligible to work in Ireland/EU. For more information, please contact **David Coyle** at **01 635 1748** or email **david@methodius.com**

title: Legal Entity Analyst, International Controllership
description: **Minimum qualifications:** * Bachelor's degree in Accounting or Finance, or equivalent practical experience. * Professional qualification CIMA/ACA/ACCA or equivalent. * 4 years of experience in accounting or a related function, or 1 year with an advanced degree in accounting, business, or finance. **Preferred qualifications:** * Experience managing project planning, status reporting, timeline estimation, issue tracking, meeting facilitation, and project documentation. * Experience in agreement and spend governance. * Experience in SQL/ERP and ability to have strong attention to detail, organizational skills and focus on continuous improvement. * Ability to propose next steps and roadblock solutions and anticipate questions and challenges early, manage time, and follow work plans. * Ability to have a collaborative style and work with cross\-functional and international teams. * Excellent problem\-solving skills and ability to communicate with executive management. **About The Job** The international controllership seeks to ensure that any Alphabet company can do any business in any country. In order to realise this goal and operate in a responsible and controlled manner it is imperative to take a risk based approach and to align resources with jurisdictional and regional requirements. This organization therefore forms an integral part of Alphabet’s global controllership, working closely with our global operations team, professional service providers, in\-country business leaders, and the broader finance, tax and legal teams. We manage the Legal Entity processes that allow Google to expand and operate globally, as well as ensuring an optimal global corporate landscape. As part of the Legal Entity Governance team, your work will be a critical coordination point, directly supporting the creation of new legal entities around the world. You will be assessing the overall legal entity landscape to ensure any dormant entities are assessed for liquidation. You will partner closely with various business areas across multiple product areas including Finance Engineering, Controllership, and other finance and on finance teams across Google to ensure our operations and corporate landscape assessments run smoothly. The name Google came from "googol," a mathematical term for the number 1 followed by 100 zeros. And nobody at Google loves big numbers like the Finance team when providing in depth analysis on all manner of strategic decisions across Google products. From developing forward\-thinking analysis to generating management reports to scaling our automated financial processes, the Finance organization is an important partner and advisor to the business. **Responsibilities** * Examine entity data to evaluate Alphabet's worldwide footprint, ensuring an optimal organizational structure and supporting the implementation of legal entity policies across all Product Areas (PAs) as businesses expand or business needs change. * Manage legal entity data and workflows directly within Introspect, using the platform to deliver operational updates on the progress of new entities and liquidations. * Coordinate with various finance and non\-finance teams across Alphabet to facilitate legal entity set up, operationalization and liquidation. * Pinpoint root causes and suggests actionable improvements for legal entity processes, employing technology to enhance both accuracy and efficiency. * Generate detailed project management materials for entity creation, such as timeline estimates, status reports, and issue tracking. Resolve Project Roadblocks, work with stakeholders to overcome obstacles and ensure successful outcomes for assigned portions of cross\-functional projects. Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

title: Machine Operator
company: Harmony Vale Produce T/A Clarkes Fresh Fruit
description: Clarke’s Fresh Fruit—an award\-winning leader in Ireland’s fresh produce industry—operates from a state\-of\-the\-art family farm in Co. Meath and is now seeking enthusiastic Machine Operators to join our growing team for immediate start. As a Machine Operator, you will play a key role in supporting our high\-performance production environment. Reporting to the Supervisor and/or Warehouse Manager, you will carry out daily tasks with efficiency, accuracy, and pride, ensuring our renowned standards of Hygiene, Food Safety, and Health \& Safety are consistently upheld. You’ll help us meet production targets while identifying and reporting any issues that may arise—contributing directly to the continued success and quality Clarke’s Fresh Fruit is known for. If you’re motivated, reliable, and eager to be part of a dynamic team within one of Ireland’s top fresh produce companies, we’d love to hear from you. **\#NOTE: This is a machine operator position in a fresh produce environment, working on a production line.** **Key Duties \& Responsibilities** * Set up production lines to ensure they are ready for efficient operation. * Monitor the production line to ensure smooth running and maintain a consistent supply of product being fed onto the line. * Support warehouse operations including loading/unloading produce, picking orders, wrapping pallets, and checking accompanying paperwork. * Follow instructions and daily work plans provided by your supervisor and/or Warehouse Manager. * Maintain a clean, tidy, and safe working environment always around the production line. * Demonstrate flexibility by moving between production lines as needed. * Fulfil customer orders to the highest quality standards. * Maintain excellent accuracy and attention to detail, particularly when picking customer orders. **Key Behaviours/Competencies** * Ability to work overtime and weekends. * Hard\-working, motivated, flexible, and able to perform well under pressure. * Experience in the food industry and/or previous machine operation experience is a strong advantage. * Eagerness to grow and develop — with real opportunities for progression within the company. * Good IT skills: experience with ERP systems is a distinct advantage. **Benefits:** * Paid Weekly * Bonus Scheme incentive * Bike to work scheme * On\-site parking **Ability to commute/relocate:** * Stamullen, Co. Meath: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) **Work authorisation:** * Must be eligible to work in Ireland (required) **Work Location**: In person (Stamullen) Job Type: Full\-time Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * On\-site parking Ability to commute/relocate: * Stamullin, County Meath: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Application question(s): * Are you currently working or living in Ireland Education: * Junior Certificate (preferred) Experience: * Machine operation: 1 year (required) Licence/Certification: * Forklift Licence (preferred) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Marketing Compliance Specialist
description: **About us** At Davy, it’s the unique talents of all our people that have been the foundations of our success for 100 years. As we continue to grow, so do you. Because you are not just part of our team – you are a key player in shaping our future. At Davy, you are the difference. Established in 1926, the Davy Group sits at the heart of wealth and capital in Ireland, providing a broad range of services to high\-net\-worth individuals, self\-directed investors, small\-to\-medium enterprises, credit unions, corporations, and domestic \& international institutional investors. Our culture is shaped around three values, which are at the heart of everything we do: client success, one Davy, and building a proud legacy. Sharing the same goals, we work together to deliver exceptional outcomes for our clients. As part of a team of over 900, you will be encouraged to learn and to grow, both professionally and personally. **About the role** An exciting opportunity has opened up in our Strategy, Brand and Marketing department. In the role, the successful candidate will: * Be accountable for the design and operationalisation of systems which assure the quality, completeness and regulatory compliance of our marketing communications. Relevant regulations include MiFID II, the CBI Consumer Protection Code 2025 and the Data Protection Act (2018\). * Will require engagement with, and co\-ordination of, multiple stakeholders across our marketing, wealth and capital markets team, as well as the maintenance of close working relationships with risk and compliance colleagues. * The role will report to the Head of Marketing Communications within the Strategy, Brand and Marketing team but operate cross\-functionally. **Requirements** **About you** We are looking for somebody who has the following background and skills: * Direct experience of the relevant regulatory frameworks; * Experience in a similar or related role in financial services in Ireland and/ or the EU; * Ability to interpret regulatory requirements both practically and effectively; * Strong inter\-personal skills and stakeholder management experience; * Excellent communication skills with strong attention to detail. The role is being recruited at a senior associate level, which typically equates to between 3 and 5 years experience in a directly relevant role. * **Benefits** **What we offer** At Davy, our people are our biggest investment. In today’s fast\-moving world, we know it’s important to feel secure and empowered. That’s why Davy benefits support you through all of life’s stages, offering health and wellness, flexible working options, flexible benefits allowance, learning and development opportunities, through to assisted retirement planning. **Health and Wellbeing** Psychological safety, emotional wellbeing and support are a priority at Davy. We offer a comprehensive Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) and mental health first aid. Our Sports \& Social teams promote physical wellbeing and social connection. **Reward and Recognition** We offer a competitive remuneration package to include salary, bonus, pension and a host of additional benefits to protect you and your family when it matters most. **Growth and Development** Our award\-winning L\&D function supports you and your growth at Davy. We offer a comprehensive range of professional and personal development opportunities. Our culture supports learning, and we encourage you to explore more at Davy. **Positive Workplace** Our workplace is inclusive, diverse, and offers everyone a sense of belonging. We are flexible and continue to support a hybrid working model. You will get to be your best at Davy. **Values and Culture** Exceptional client outcomes delivered by a world class team, underpinned by our core Davy values – One Team, Client Success, and Proud Legacy. **Important Information** The position set out above is defined as a “Controlled Function (CF)” role, namely CF4 under Section 20 of the Central Bank Reform Act 2010 and Schedule 1 of the Regulations (S.I. No. 437 of 2011\) as amended. In accordance with Section 21 of the Central Bank Reform Act 2010 and the Fitness and Probity Standards 2014, any offer of employment is conditional upon you demonstrating that you meet the Fitness \& Probity requirements, applicable to this position. The role is also subject to the Minimum Competency Code and therefore there is a requirement to hold or be willing to take the APA (Regulation \& Investment) or (Pensions) or Registered Stockbroker or QFA exams. Davy is an equal opportunities employer, committed to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace. We value diversity in both background and experience, and even if you don’t meet all of the requirements outlined in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. We review all applications received. As part of our commitment to creating an accessible environment, we want to ensure that everyone has an equal opportunity to participate in the interview process. If you require any reasonable accommodations, please let us know, and we will do our best to make the necessary arrangements. Appointment to this role is subject to the candidate's eligibility to work in Ireland. Where agency assistance is required, our Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to us by recruitment agencies will not be accepted for this role. Davy Group is a member of the Bank of Ireland Group.

title: Mechanical QA/QC Engineer
description: **This Role is based in Germany\- Rotation Plan available** Fohntech Group is seeking a Junior Mechanical QA/QC Engineer to join their team in Germany. The successful candidate will possess a blend of technical and soft skills required to succeed, have technical knowledge of mechanical engineering and project management as well as the communication skills necessary to interact with the project and client teams. **Responsibilities** * To support the engineering requirements through managing \& liaising with Key Project Stakeholders (Client design team, Main contractor, Suppliers, Sub\- contractors, etc). * Familiarisation of Design/IFC specifications and drawings to fully understand the scope of the Project works. * Contributing to the Co\-ordination process to transform design information into full installation drawings by BIM personnel ensuring spec compliance and as per schedule priority. * Familiarisation with the Project schedule and ensuring Engineering activities support the construction build schedule (e.g. Procurement, etc). * Creation and issue of technical submittals, tracking of approval for same. * Engaging vendors for both technical support and value engineering solutions. * Management of the material procurement utilising Engineering Materials Report (EMR) by producing material take offs identifying the lead in periods and field need rates * Management of equipment procurement and tracking for delivery to site. * Ensure Works are installed in accordance with Project specifications and to the Project QA plan (for e.g. pre\-agreed benchmarking). * Completing site audits to verify that proper processes and procedures are being followed. * Preparation and agreeing project completion criteria and close out documents. * Preparing Sub\-contract and Supplier Tender Packages in conjunction with Project QS. * Manage work packs from development to implementation and handover. * Manage BIM priorities i.e. isometrics generation and release. * Liaising with clients and writing up reports to present to Directors and other relevant parties. * Scheduling meetings and training sessions when necessary. * Coordinating with Client project Mgt. teams and contractors to ensure that designs meet legal and regulatory requirements. * Providing technical support to construction team during the building phase of projects. **Candidate Requirements** * 2 years' experience working with a recognized Mechanical/ contractor in process mechanical / pipework. * Excellent organizational and project planning skills. * Ability to work as part of a team. * Excellent negotiation, problem solving and decision\-making skills. * Proficient in use of Navisworks and Database systems related to Engineering functions. * Experience with Project/Primavera planning software and CAD/BIM process. * Ability to manage workload and prioritise. **Next Steps** If you are interested in this role, please apply directly or email your CV to careers@fohntechgroup.com ### **Job Information** Date Opened 04/30/2026 Job Type Full time Industry Construction City Dublin 15 State/Province Dublin Country Ireland Zip/Postal Code D15

title: Mechanical Site Fitter
description: **The Role** You will work as part of our multi\-disciplinary engineering team to bring water and wastewater projects from initial design, through to completion and handover to the client. You will have the opportunity to work alongside, and collaborate with, industry\-leading project teams, working in a one\-team culture while broadening your own skill set. As a Site Mechanical Fitter, you will be expected to support the project manager and contracts manager in the daily operation and delivery of projects as well as showing progression and development in your own career. You will primarily be involved in Mechanical, Electrical, Instrumentation, Control and Automation (MEICA) installation work however due to the nature of the works completed by Glanua you will also have an opportunity to witness civil/building engineering works as well as commissioning and operations. This will present a wide multi\-disciplinary engineering knowledge base and an opportunity to progress your career. Glanua is a progressive company that always seeks to develop its employees. When you demonstrate aptitude and interest in furthering your career within the organisation, training and promotion will always be available to you. **Main Duties and Responsibilities:** The main duties and responsibilities of the Site Mechanical Fitter are outlined as follows: * Work as part of a team in the delivery of one or more projects at any one time, embracing the ethos of a “one team” culture. * Ensure Health, Safety and Environmental standards, policies and procedures are always adhered to. * Review of design stage mechanical drawings and P\&ID in order to validate designs, familiarise with project and spot issues before they arise. * Site install of mechanical systems, pipework and equipment including supporting brackets and stands. Pipework and valve installations may range from DN4mm to DN2000mm in PVC, DI, PE or SS. * Modification, fabrication and installation of mechanical systems. * Complete system testing in the form of pipework pressure tests and completion of inspection test records (ITRs) on completion of installation. * Maintenance and repair of existing water / wastewater treatment equipment including strip\-down, part replacement and re\-building. * Troubleshoot equipment and instrumentation, strip\-down and repair equipment as necessary to ensure good working order. * Conduct periodic inspections and preventative maintenance on system installations. * Organise and facilitate plant shutdowns and/or diversion of flows as required with project delivery team and the customer through permit\-to\-work systems. * Ensuring all works are to the company and mechanical standards. * Complete Health \& Safety checks and permits as required for work activities being undertaken. * Ensure a positive experience for the customer and their representatives instilling an ethos of professionalism, collaboration and co\-operation. * Seek to improve the mechanical installation process by providing lessons learned feedback and innovative solutions for adoption on future projects. * Keep appraised of the latest standards and technology through continuous professional development (CPD). * Show an eagerness to learn and progress in your own career as well as the mentoring and training of more junior staff. Glanua has a proven track record of developing staff and promoting from within the organisation. * Other duties as required from time to time. **Knowledge, Skills and Experience:** * The main knowledge, skills and experience required of the Site Mechanical Fitter are outlined as follows: * Minimum of 2 years’ experience working in a similar role, preferably in the water and wastewater industry. * Trade Qualified Mechanical Fitter/Fabricator, Maintenance Mechanical Fitter. * Experienced in the mechanical installation of equipment, systems and pipework (including Stainless Steel, PVC, PE, and Ductile Iron). * Good working knowledge of mechanical systems including strip\-down repair and re\-building. * An interest in developing a career within an industry\-leading organisation. * Ability to work as part of a team in project delivery and provide input at team meetings. * Ability to engage with suppliers and sub\-contractors at procurement stage and in the delivery of their scope of supply during contract delivery. * Ability to use own initiative, problem solve and adjust as issues arise during project delivery. * Strong communication skills with a high level of motivation. * Full driving licence **What we offer you:** * The opportunity to join an ambitious and growing organisation. * Monday to Friday working week finishing early on Fridays. * 22 days annual leave plus 10 bank holidays. * Competitive base salary with * A company laptop and phone. * Access to EAP – 24/7 365 days confidential employee counselling service, free to avail for employees and their families. **About Glanua** At Glanua, we believe that people are the core of the business. We know that to build successful creative teams we need a diverse workforce that can deliver new innovative ways of thinking. We provide an environment where you will have the freedom to develop and grow to your full potential. Glanua is an industry leader in providing innovative and sustainable engineering solutions to design, build, operate and maintain critical water and wastewater infrastructure across Ireland and the UK. We are a trusted provider to the municipal and industrial sector where our clients include Ireland and the UK’s largest water utilities, Uisce Éireann (formally Irish Water), Thames Water, Yorkshire Water and Affinity Water. Glanua is passionate about solving today’s environmental challenges and delivering a decarbonised and sustainable world. We know that to build successful creative teams we need a diverse workforce that can deliver new innovative ways of thinking. We provide an environment where you will have the freedom to develop and grow to your full potential. We champion our core values and uphold the highest standards of Safety and Well\-being, Innovation, Integrity, Collaboration, Courage, Agility, Accountability, Diversity and Inclusion in everything we do. We have a talented team of 550\+ people across Ireland and the UK and are rapidly expanding our workforce across several disciplines to meet our ambitious growth plans.

title: National Centre Administrative Officer

title: Operational Risk Associate
description: Pantheon has been at the forefront of private markets investing for more than 40 years, earning a reputation for an innovative approach to investing in secondaries, co\-investments, and primary fund investments, as well as capital formation across commingled funds, evergreen vehicles and customized solutions. Our specialist investment capabilities span multiple strategies across private equity, infrastructure and real assets, and private credit. Through our collaborative and committed culture, we find new ways to solve complex problems together and deliver innovative investment opportunities across private markets. Pantheon currently manages approximately $82\.3 billion in AUM across all its strategies, serving more than 750 institutional and 638 private wealth clients worldwide For further details please visit www.pantheon.com **Purpose of Role** The Operational Risk Associate will be part of the wider Enterprise Risk / Operational Risk function and support the team in further developing and managing operational risks across all business functions. The position will report to the Head of Enterprise/Operational Risk. **Key Responsibilities** * Support the development and implementation of Pantheon’s global operational risk frameworks and policies and procedures for adoption by Pantheon Group entities * Support the ISAE 3402/SSAE 16 annual processes including completion of the document, drafting of controls wording and providing external auditor with supporting information. * Support process and control reviews to identify risks and issues in Pantheon’s control environment. Reviews will include identified areas of risk, monitoring programme and new business processes. * Manage Error/Incident reporting process including reporting to management and resolution of any issues. Ensure monitoring procedures are in line with Pantheon’s risk policies. Direct investigation of serious error or breaches and make recommendations for change to management * Co\-ordinate and assist in providing risk oversight and governance across third party supplier relationships of all Pantheon Group entities * Support business continuity process including BCP and testing programme. * Support to manage various risk\-related frameworks such as the data privacy / data protection, vendor risk, Insurance risk, and assist with any client / business related queries * Support and further develop a centralised library of processes, risks and controls in a strategic risk system **Skills And Experience Required** * Degree level education * Experience of working in a risk function ideally in an investment banking, asset management, private equity environment * Understanding of investment management and products * Ability to work on own initiative and in team * Ability to identify when issues should be escalated for further discussion * Controls awareness * Keen problem\-solving skills, ability to offer solutions to issues **This job description is not to be construed as an exhaustive statement of duties, responsibilities, or requirements. You may be required to perform other job\-related duties as reasonably requested by your manager.** **Pantheon is an Equal Opportunities employer, we are committed to building a diverse and inclusive workforce so if you're excited about this role but your past experience doesn't perfectly align we'd still encourage you to apply.**

title: Operations Support Officer
description: **Company Description** SAS Connect is currently looking to recruit an Operations Support Officer to work in Flight Support. You will work closely with the Flight Operations Engineer and the Flight Operations department, providing support to the operation and crew. This role will require close collaboration with wider group functions, including group Flight Ops Engineering, EFB and IT operations. Based in our Head Office in Swords Business Campus. The position is targeted at a candidate who is a recent college graduate. **Main Tasks and Responsibilities** * Responsible for testing iPad OS and Application version updates and EFB changes on the crew device. * Assist in the administration of operational systems, including data management, onboarding and offboarding processes. * Maintain support documentation, processes and guidance materials within the Flight Operations dept. * Provide support and assist crew with EFB and operational IT issues. * Issue bulletins and other communication to crew for EFB and IT changes. * Assist in continuous improvement projects and system migrations within Flight Operations. * Assist in the regulatory approval process for changes within Flight Operations. * Assist the Flight Operations dept in the continuous compliance of air operations regulations. **Education** * Hold a degree from a third\-level institution, ideally Beng in Aeronautical Engineering * Prior experience in project work in an aviation\-related field is beneficial but not essential. **The ideal candidate would:** * Possess a technical mindset and be comfortable with digital tools and systems. * Be a confident and effective communicator. * Possess an eagerness and curiosity to learn. * Be detailed\-oriented and organised. * Have an interest in IT and technical processes. * Have an interest in broadening your knowledge in Airline and IT Operations. Applicants must be eligible to live and work in the EU without restriction. If you are innovative and enthusiastic and wish to join our dynamic and diverse Team, please send your CV and cover letter via LinkedIn. Applications for this position close 06th May 2026\. We thank all applicants for their interest. However, only those whose background and experience match the requirements of the role will be contacted.

title: PSDP / Safety Professional
description: **PSDP / Safety Professional** **\*Dublin – Great Company in an Excellent Location\*** I’m currently working with a leading multidisciplinary Engineering consultancy based in Dublin with offices in Ireland, UK, Australia who are looking for a **PSDP / Safety Professional** with at least 5 years’ experience to join their growing team. My client pride themselves with a broad range of projects in civil, structural, transportation, mechanical and electrical engineering. They work with a large number of government agencies, local authorities, private sector and blue chip clients. As **PSDP / Safety Professional** you will work collaboratively with the team and ensure the company's roles on a project are discharged and develop a positive and active safety culture on their projects across large\-scale residential, commercial and infrastructure projects. As PSDP you will ensure discharge of the role of PSDP on construction projects, attending client/design and site meetings. You will compile and prepare preliminary safety and health plans. **Sample Projects:** * Modern high rise office buildings – Over 8 floors in Dublin * Residential developments – Various 2, 3 \& 4 storey units and high rise apartment blocks * Education – Multiple schools around the Leinster area * Government and Public buildings * Hotels – Various hotel developments in Dublin * Retail – Various retail developments around Ireland **Requirements:** * Minimum Level 8 degree in Civil/Structural Engineering or equivalent. * PSDP / Safety Professional with a minimum of 5 years post graduate experience, working with a consultancy working as a PSDP on projects in Ireland or the UK. * Must have excellent communications skills both verbal and written * Eagerness to learn and progress **Salary:** * Excellent Salary (circa €70k – €80k\+) doe * Excellent benefits package – full details available on application. * Paid membership fees to two professional institutes * Life Assurance \& Permanent Health Insurance * Paid Health Insurance * Pension * Annual Bonus * Profit Share * 24 days leave that increases with loyalty * Great location with access to sports courts * Excellent CPD If you are a **PSDP / Safety Professional** considering a career move, please feel to send your CV or contact Alice Condon for a confidential discussion. GPC will not send any applicants cv’s to clients without a full discussion and prior consent. 17925

title: Partnerships Operations Executive
description: We’re working with a leading residential development platform to appoint a Partnerships Operations Associate within its Partnerships function. This role supports the delivery of large‑scale housing initiatives across Ireland, acting as a key coordination point between internal teams and external partners. You’ll work closely with a Senior Manager to support planning, coordination, and stakeholder engagement across B2G, B2C, and joint venture delivery channels. It’s ideal for someone who enjoys working in a structured, fast‑paced environment and wants to build experience across public and private sector housing partnerships. Key Responsibilities: * Support the coordination of partnership activities, including project timelines and key milestones * Help manage day‑to‑day communication and information flow with internal teams such as development, delivery, finance, and legal * Assist in coordinating meetings, documentation, and follow‑up actions with external partners including government departments, Approved Housing Bodies, and local authorities * Support the management of joint venture relationships and basic tracking of key deliverables * Help maintain trackers, dashboards, and reporting templates to monitor partnership progress and risks * Contribute to improving internal processes and systems within the partnerships function About You: * 5\+ years’ experience in the built environment (for example, real estate, housing, infrastructure, or development) * Familiarity with working alongside or within public sector or B2G environments (e.g. local authorities, housing agencies, AHBs, or related bodies) * Strong organisational and administrative skills, with the ability to juggle multiple priorities * Comfortable using basic project management or CRM tools and working with spreadsheets and reports * Good written and verbal communication skills, with experience drafting clear emails, briefs, and summaries * Someone who is proactive, detail‑oriented, and keen to develop a career in strategic partnerships and housing delivery This is a great opportunity to grow your experience in housing partnerships and play a visible role in delivering impactful housing projects across Ireland. For a confidential discussion click Apply Now or contact cogrady@elevatepartners.ie

title: Pastry Chef
description: At Killashee we believe in bringing together the most talented hospitality professionals to work together and provide wonderful guest experiences. We hire self\-motivated people, train them to do their job superbly and create a working environment where they can flourish and develop their careers. We now have a fantastic new opportunity for a **Pastry Chef** to join our team and contribute to the future success of Killashee Hotel. **Key Requirements:** * Creative and passionate about culinary arts * Excellent communication skills * Self\-motivated and have positive attitude * Flexible with hours/ own transport * Strong interpersonal, leadership, time management and organisational skills * Full working rights in Ireland. **Main Duties:** * To prepare and produce food for the area, which you are assigned, and to the standards set by the Head Pastry Chef. * To ensure consistency of product at all times. * To adhere to the 7 principles of the HACCP in all\-cleaning storage, preparation and working of food. * To always work in a friendly and professional manner. * To always work as part of an overall team. * To be responsible for the supervision of Junior Chef de partie and commis chefs. **Why work with us:** **At Killashee Hotel we believe that exceptional guest experiences start with an exceptional team. We are proud to foster a supportive, dynamic, and rewarding workplace within the hospitality industry. As a valued member of our team, you will benefit from:** * Professional Growth \& Development: Ongoing training and upskilling opportunities to support your career progression. External \& Internal programmes provided. * Complimentary Leisure Centre Membership: Enjoy full access to our leisure facilities to help you stay active and well. Includes access to all offered classes, use of sauna, jacuzzi and swimming pool. * Exclusive Employee Rates: Discounted stays at FBD Hotels \& Resorts \- perfect for your well\-deserved breaks. Five hotels in Ireland and 2 hotels in Spain. * Health \& Wellbeing Support: Access to our Employee Assistance Programme and a range of wellness initiatives to support your overall wellbeing. * Insurance Discounts: Affinity rates on home, car, and travel insurance through FBD Insurance. * Free On\-Site Parking: Hassle\-free and secure parking available for all team members. * Meals Provided on Duty: Fresh and nutritious meals provided while you work. Facilities for unlimited amount of tea and coffee during the shift hours. * Employee Referral Programme: Recommend great people and earn rewards through our referral incentive scheme. * Team Social Events: Regular events that bring our team together to relax and connect outside of work. From summer parties, staff sports days to team building activities, and not forgetting annual Christmas party. * Flexible Scheduling: Shift patterns designed to support a healthy work\-life balance. * Positive Team Culture: A welcoming, inclusive and diverse environment that values teamwork, respect, and recognition. Fostering positive work culture through living by companies' core values. * Recognition Programmes: Celebrate achievements and milestones through our dedicated team recognition initiatives. Monthly staff recognition celebration alongside our GEM awards, Quarterly Manager awards and other recognition for individual or team milestones. *At Killashee we thrive to succeed in all areas of the hotel by incorporating our company values, as a team we think about the guest in everything we do, we treat each other with respect, we work cohesively as one team, we are creative and think outside the box and finally we have a can\-do approach and take accountability.*
